---
title: "HD vs MM – scRNA-seq CD8⁺ T Cell Analysis"
subtitle: "Integrated multi-sample reanalysis"
author: 
  - name: Jordan Dutel
    affiliation: 
      - CRCT – CHU Toulouse
      - GENIM Team
    email: jordan.dutel@inserm.fr
date: today
date-format: "DD MMMM YYYY"

lang: en
title-block-banner: true
title-block-style: default

format:
  html:
    theme:
      light: cosmo
      dark: darkly
    # theme: superhero         # Bootstrap
    css: styles.css
    toc: true
    toc-title: Table of Contents
    toc-depth: 7
    toc-location: left
    toc-expand: 2
    number-sections: true
    number-depth: 7
    page-layout: full
    smooth-scroll: true
    anchor-sections: true
    section-divs: true
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show code"
    code-tools: true
    code-copy: true
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-block-bg: true
    code-block-border-left: "#31BAE9"
    highlight-style: github
    embed-resources: true
    fig-responsive: true
    fig-format: retina
    fig-align: center
    fig-dpi: 300
    df-print: paged
    other-links:
      - text: Thomas's PPT
        icon: filetype-ppt
        href: ../00_context/PPT_Thomas_BCL6_09012026.pdf 
    
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
  error: false
  # cache: true
  # freeze: auto
  eval: true

editor: visual
---

```{r Setup, echo=FALSE}
############
# PACKAGES #
############

# BiocManager::install("SingleCellExperiment")
# install.packages("devtools")
# BiocManager::install("DropletUtils")
# devtools::install_github("immunogenomics/presto")
# BiocManager::install("scDblFinder")

library(DropletUtils)
library(SingleCellExperiment)
library(scater)
library(scran)
library(qs)
library(Seurat)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(clustree)
library(harmony)
library(patchwork)
library(dplyr)
library(ggridges)
library(readr)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(SingleR)
library(celldex)
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(circlize)
library(presto)
library(tidyverse)
library(tibble)
library(matrixStats)
library(viridis)
library(scuttle)
library(scDblFinder)
library(ggbeeswarm)
library(here)
library(scales)
library(Polychrome)
library(future)
library(glmGamPoi)
library(lisi)
library(pheatmap)
library(cluster)
library(ggrepel)
library(rstatix)
library(ggpubr)
library(pals)
library(IRanges)
library(sp)
library(SummarizedExperiment)
library(furrr)
library(fgsea)
library(msigdbr)
library(Seurat)
library(edgeR)
library(gt)
library(DT)




###########
# OPTIONS #
###########

options(
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  scipen = 999
)

options(future.globals.maxSize = +Inf)

plan(sequential)

#########
# PATHS #
#########

here::i_am("04_report/HD_vs_MM_scRNAseq_CD8_Tcells_analysis.qmd")
# here::i_am("02_script/HD_vs_MM_scRNAseq_reanalysis.qmd")

# knitr::opts_knit$set(
#   root.dir = "~/Documents/Project/Thomas/20260116_scRNAseq_HD_MM_reanalysis")

seurat_path_Tcell_obj <- "/mnt/crct-share/crct13/PPA/PPA_work/project/01_HCvsMM/01A_HCvsMM_10x/results/Tcells_analysis/seurat_objects/QCed_seurat_Tcells_clean.qs"

# DIR_RAW    <- here("01_raw")
DIR_DATA   <- here("01_data")
# DIR_PROCESSED_DATA <- here("02_processed_data")
DIR_SCRIPT <- here("02_script")
DIR_RESULT <- here("03_result")
DIR_REPORT <- here("04_report")
DIR_ENV <- here("05_environment")
DIR_LOG <- here("06_log")
# DIR_QC     <- here("03_result", "QC")

dir.create(here(DIR_DATA, "Processed_data"),
           recursive = TRUE, 
           showWarnings = FALSE)



##########
# COLORS #
##########

colors_30 <- c(
  "#E41A1C", "#377EB8", "#4DAF4A", "#984EA3", "#FF7F00",
  "#A65628", "#F781BF", "#999999", "#66C2A5", "#FC8D62",
  "#8DA0CB", "#E78AC3", "#A6D854", "#FFD92F", "#E5C494",
  "#B3B3B3", "#1B9E77", "#D95F02", "#7570B3", "#E7298A",
  "#66A61E", "#E6AB02", "#A6761D", "#666666", "#8DD3C7",
  "#BEBADA", "#FB8072", "#80B1D3", "#FDB462", "#B3DE69"
)
colors_55 <- c(
  "#E41A1C","#377EB8","#4DAF4A","#984EA3","#FF7F00",
  "#A65628","#F781BF","#999999","#66C2A5","#FC8D62",
  "#8DA0CB","#E78AC3","#A6D854","#FFD92F","#E5C494",
  "#1B9E77","#D95F02","#7570B3","#E7298A","#66A61E",
  "#E6AB02","#A6761D","#666666","#8DD3C7","#BEBADA",
  "#FB8072","#80B1D3","#FDB462","#B3DE69","#FCCDE5",
  "#D9D9D9","#BC80BD","#CCEBC5","#FFED6F","#1F78B4",
  "#33A02C","#FB9A99","#E31A1C","#FDBF6F","#CAB2D6",
  "#6A3D9A","#FFFF99","#B15928","#8E0152","#DE77AE",
  "#7FBC41","#4EB265","#4393C3","#2166AC","#B2182B",
  "#F4A582","#92C5DE","#FDDBC7","#D1E5F0","#542788"
)

# 22 col palette
pal <-c('#e6194B', '#3cb44b', '#ffe119', '#4363d8', '#f58231', '#911eb4', '#42d4f4', '#f032e6', '#bfef45', '#fabed4', '#9A6324', '#469990', '#dcbeff', '#fffac8', '#800000', '#aaffc3', '#808000', '#ffd8b1', '#000075', '#a9a9a9', '#ffffff', '#000000')

Glasbey = glasbey.colors(32)
# swatch(Glasbey)



# Define batch variable, if modalities in batch var are <= 22 use pal, if < 30 use colors_30, otherwise use colors_55
batch_var <- "emulsion"
num_batches <- 42 # replace with actual number of batches in your dataset
if (num_batches <= 22) {
  pal <- pal
} else if (num_batches <= 30) {
  pal <- colors_30
} else {
  pal <- colors_55
}







# --- Sanity check ---
cat("Project root:", here(), "\n")
```

<br>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<br>

# Tcells

The global T cell object had been previously quality-controlled and preprocessed. It was therefore loaded directly for downstream analysis. Theis initial object come from PPA.

Standard preprocessing steps were applied, including normalization, variable feature selection, scaling, principal component analysis (PCA), and UMAP embedding, to enable visualization and subsequent subsetting of the CD8⁺ T cell compartment.

```{r Load Tcells, cache.lazy = FALSE}
# Load Total Tcells
seu <- qread(seurat_path_Tcell_obj)

seu <- seu %>%
  NormalizeData() %>%
  FindVariableFeatures() %>%
  ScaleData(features = rownames(seu)) %>%
  RunPCA(features = VariableFeatures(.)) %>%
  RunUMAP(dims = 1:20)
cat("There is ", length(Cells(seu)),  " Tcells in the dataset.")

# Save qs object
qs::qsave(
  seu,
  file = file.path(
    DIR_DATA,
    "Processed_data",
    "seu_Tcells.qs")
)

# --- Cleanup: remove scale.data to save memory ---
DefaultAssay(seu) <- "RNA"
seu[["RNA"]]@scale.data <- matrix(0, 0, 0)  # removes scaled data
invisible(gc())  # forces garbage collection to free RAM
```

::: callout-important
The scale.data() slot consumes a substantial amount of memory and can significantly increase object size. In most workflows, it is not required, except for specific downstream analyses such as gene signature scoring or certain dimensionality reduction methods. To optimize memory usage and prevent unnecessary RAM overhead, it is recommended to remove the scale.data slot when not explicitly needed. It can be recomputed at any stage if required.
:::

To optimize memory usage, we examined the size of the Seurat object and its individual slots.

The `scale.data` slot represented a substantial proportion of the total object size and was not required for downstream analyses. It was therefore removed to reduce memory footprint.

```{r Load raw data, cache.lazy = FALSE}
seu <- qs::qread(
  file.path(
    DIR_DATA,
    "Processed_data",
    "seu_Tcells.qs")
    # "seu_Tcells.rds")
)

size_table <- tibble(
  Slot = c("Total object", "RNA assay", "ADT assay",
           "Metadata", "RNA@data", "RNA@counts", "RNA@scale.data"),
  Size_GB = c(
    object.size(seu),
    object.size(seu@assays$RNA),
    object.size(seu@assays$ADT),
    object.size(seu@meta.data),
    object.size(seu@assays$RNA@data),
    object.size(seu@assays$RNA@counts),
    object.size(seu@assays$RNA@scale.data)
  )
) %>%
  mutate(Size_GB = format(Size_GB, units = "GB"))

size_table

sizes <- list(
  cells        = length(Cells(seu)),
  object       = format(object.size(seu), units = "GB"),
  RNA_assay    = format(object.size(seu@assays$RNA), units = "GB"),
  ADT_assay    = format(object.size(seu@assays$ADT), units = "GB"),
  metadata     = format(object.size(seu@meta.data), units = "GB"),
  RNA_data     = format(object.size(seu@assays$RNA@data), units = "GB"),
  RNA_counts   = format(object.size(seu@assays$RNA@counts), units = "GB"),
  RNA_scale    = format(object.size(seu@assays$RNA@scale.data), units = "GB")
)

cat(sprintf(
"Dataset summary
----------------
Number of T cells : %s
Total object size : %s

Assay sizes:
- RNA assay       : %s
- ADT assay       : %s
- Metadata        : %s
- RNA@data        : %s
- RNA@counts      : %s
- RNA@scale.data  : %s
",
sizes$cells,
sizes$object,
sizes$RNA_assay,
sizes$ADT_assay,
sizes$metadata,
sizes$RNA_data,
sizes$RNA_counts,
sizes$RNA_scale
))

DefaultAssay(seu) <- "RNA"
seu[["RNA"]]@scale.data <- matrix(0, 0, 0)  # supprime l’ancien scaled data
invisible(gc())  # force garbage collection pour libérer la RAM
```

The distribution of ADT expression for CD8a-TotalSeqB was examined, as this marker reliably identifies CD8⁺ T cells.

A threshold was defined to distinguish CD8⁺ from CD4⁺ T cells.

To support cutoff selection, the `predicted.celltype.l1` annotation from the previously generated PPA object was used as a reference.

```{r Cutoff CD8 subset Viz}
VlnPlot(seu, 
        features = "CD8a-TotalSeqB", 
        cols = pal,
        assay = "ADT", pt.size = 0, 
        group.by = "id") + 
  ggtitle("CD8a-TotalSeqB (ADT)") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 2, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```
```{r Cutoff CD8 subset Viz 2}
VlnPlot(seu, 
        features = "CD8a-TotalSeqB",
        cols = pal, 
        assay = "ADT", pt.size = 0, 
        group.by = "predicted.celltype.l1") + 
  ggtitle("CD8a-TotalSeqB (ADT)") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 2, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```
```{r Cutoff CD8 subset Viz 3}
hist(seu@assays$ADT@data["CD8a-TotalSeqB", ],
     breaks = 50,
     main = "Distribution of CD8-TotalSeqB (ADT) expression",
     xlab = "CD8-TotalSeqB (ADT) expression",
     ylab = "Frequency") 
abline(v = 2, lty = "dashed", col = "red")
```

CD8a-TotalSeqB ADT expression displayed a bimodal distribution, enabling the definition of a threshold to distinguish CD8⁺ from CD4⁺ T cells.

Cutoff selection was supported by the `predicted.celltype.l1` annotation, allowing concordance between protein-level signal and reference-based cell type assignment.

## Subsetting CD8 Tcells {.tabset}

Subsetting of CD8⁺ T cells was performed using CD8a-TotalSeqB ADT expression.

A threshold of 2 was selected based on the bimodal distribution of the signal and cross-validated with `predicted.celltype.l1` annotations, enabling robust discrimination between CD8⁺ and CD8⁻ T cells.

```{r Subset CD8+ cells}
#| cache: false

# Subset CD8 Tcells based on the ADT expression of CD8a-TotalSeqB. We set a cutoff at 2 which seems to separate well the two populations (CD8+ and CD8- Tcells) based on the distribution of the ADT expression and the predicted.celltype.l1 annotation.
DefaultAssay(seu) <- "ADT"
seu$CD8_prot_pos <- seu@assays$ADT@data["CD8a-TotalSeqB", ] > 2
cat(
  "There is ", sum(seu$CD8_prot_pos), " CD8+ Tcells based on the ADT expression of CD8a-TotalSeqB."
)


DefaultAssay(seu) <- "RNA"

seu_CD8 <- subset(
  seu,
  subset = CD8_prot_pos
)
```

```{r Save CD8+ cells subset}
remove(seu)


# Save qs object
qs::qsave(
  seu_CD8,
  file = file.path(
    DIR_DATA,
    "Processed_data",
    "HD_vs_MM_scRNAseq_reanalysis_SCE_CD8_subset.qs")
)
```

<br>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<br>

# Load CD8 Tcells {.tabset}

If not already available in memory, the CD8⁺ T cell subset was loaded for specific CD8+ Tcells analysis.

The file path to the CD8⁺ T cell subset object was then defined to enable reuse across quality control and downstream analytical scripts.

```{r Load CD8+ cells subset}
# Load qs object
seu_CD8 <- qs::qread(
  file.path(
    DIR_DATA,
    "Processed_data",
    "HD_vs_MM_scRNAseq_reanalysis_SCE_CD8_subset.qs")
)
```

<br>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<br>

# QC {.tabset}

## Strategy {.tabset}

### Methods to flag outliers/bad/low quality cells {.tabset}

::: callout-tip
Maybe try to add a second annotation to have a continuous measure of how much a cell is an outlier for a given metric.
:::

For each QC metric, 1 batch-aware annotations should be added to the metadata (***computed per emulsion***):

-   `isOutlier_metric`: logical classification obtained from `isOutlier()`. A categorical variable (*low*, *normal*, *high*) based on ±3×MAD from the batch-specific median.
-   `??? An other technic` In next analysis...

This design separates **metric computation**, **annotation**, and **filtering**, ensuring reproducibility and flexible downstream thresholding.

::: callout-important
All QC metrics and outlier detection procedures must be computed on the **raw counts matrix (`counts` slot)** !

Performing QC on normalized or scaled data introduces bias and invalidates threshold interpretation.
:::

#### Outlier Detection Method

Outlier identification relies on the `isOutlier()` function from the scuttle package. This approach:

-   Uses robust statistics (median and MAD)
-   Accounts for distributional asymmetry
-   Allows batch-wise evaluation

::: callout-tip
This is statistically more reliable than applying a single global ±3×MAD cutoff across the entire dataset.
:::

::: callout-important
Always perform outlier detection **per technical batch (emulsion)** to control for batch-driven variability. Global thresholds can remove biologically valid cells from lower-depth or distinct batches !!!
:::

#### An other technic

::: callout-note
Note developed for now but maybe in the future, we could add a second annotation to have a continuous measure of how much a cell is an outlier for a given metric. This could be done by calculating the z-score of the metric for each cell within its batch and then categorizing cells based on their z-score (e.g., z \> 3 as high outliers, z \< -3 as low outliers). Or other technic... To be continued...
:::

### Global QC Strategies

#### Stringent QC

Remove cells flag as outliers (by ≥1 method) for **at least one QC metric**.

#### Non-stringent QC

Remove cells flag as outliers by (by ≥1 method, or more next times but for now I only have 1 method) **across all selected metrics**, or across a predefined subset of *critical metrics*.

::: callout-caution
QC stringency should be driven by biologically and technically critical metrics (e.g., library size, number of detected genes, mitochondrial proportion). Applying stringent filtering uniformly across all computed metrics may result in excessive cell loss.
:::

## Pre-filtering QC {.tabset}

::: callout-important
Warning: In some nect QC step, outliers are calculated across all mixed emulsions, so the cutoff may not be appropriate for certain samples (potentially overestimating outliers in some emulsions). A more robust approach would be to calculate outliers separately for each emulsion to account for batch-specific variability in library complexity.
:::

### Cells count {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Viz cell counts 2}
features <- c("condition", "id")

dir.create(
  file.path(DIR_RESULT, 
            "CD8_Tcells",
            "QC",
            "Cell_Counts"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)


for (feature in features) {
  p <- seu_CD8@meta.data %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = .data[[feature]], fill = .data[[feature]])) +
    geom_bar() +
    scale_fill_manual(values = pal, drop = FALSE) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
    ) +
    ggtitle(paste("NCells -", feature)) +
    NoLegend()

  print(p)
  
  ggsave(
  plot = p,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Cell_Counts",
    "Before_QC",
    paste0("Cell_Counts_By_", feature, "_Before_QC_CD8_Tcells.png")),
  width = 6,
  height = 4)
}


features <- c("emulsion")

for (feature in features) {
  p <- seu_CD8@meta.data %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = .data[[feature]], fill = .data[[feature]])) +
    geom_bar() +
    scale_fill_manual(values = colors_55, drop = FALSE) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
    ) +
    ggtitle(paste("NCells -", feature)) +
    NoLegend()

  print(p)
  
  ggsave(
  plot = p,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Cell_Counts",
    "Before_QC",
    paste0("Cell_Counts_By_", feature, "_Before_QC_CD8_Tcells.png")),
  width = 6,
  height = 4)
}
```

```{r QC CD8 Viz cell counts 1}
table_interactive <- seu_CD8@meta.data %>%
  count(emulsion, condition, name = "Cells") %>%
  mutate(
    Percent_global = percent(Cells / sum(Cells), accuracy = 0.1) ) %>% 
  group_by(condition) %>%
  mutate(
    Percent_within_condition = percent(Cells / sum(Cells), accuracy = 0.1)
  ) %>%  ungroup() %>%
  rename(Batch = emulsion) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = condition,
    values_from = Percent_within_condition,
    values_fill = "0%"
  ) %>% 
  mutate(
    MM = ifelse(is.na(MM), "0%", MM),
    HD = ifelse(is.na(HD), "0%", HD),
    Total = rowSums(select(., contains("Cells")))
  ) %>%
  bind_rows(
    summarise(
      .,
      across(where(is.numeric), sum),
      Batch = "Total"
    )
  )

datatable(
  table_interactive,
  options = list(
    pageLength = 10,
    autoWidth = TRUE
  ),
  rownames = FALSE
)
```

The H25b emulsion have 85 cells... very few...but maybe not bad quality cells. We will see that later.

We cann see that we have a higher number of cells in MM (Multiple Myeloid) condition compare to HD (Healthy Donor). We need to keep this in mind for further analysis because it could biase our results if we don't take it into account.

Apparently, there is also an emulsion T23491m_NK with no cells but it is not a CD8 Tcell but an NK cell (based on the annotation and the expression of CD8a and CD8b). We will remove it from the dataset to avoid any bias in our analysis.

```{r Remove patients id "T23491m_NK"}
# Remove the patient id "T23491m_NK" which is not a CD8 Tcell but an NK cell (based on the annotation and the expression of CD8a and CD8b)
seu_CD8 <- subset(seu_CD8, subset = emulsion != "T23491m_NK")
```

Here, we look at the repartition of the cells by emulsion on the UMAP.

```{r QC CD8 Viz cell counts 3}
#| fig-width: 20
#| fig-height: 12
#| out-width: 100%


DimPlot(seu_CD8, group.by = "emulsion") +
  facet_wrap(~emulsion, ncol = 6) +
  ggtitle("UMAP by emulsion before QC CD8 Tcells") +
  NoLegend() +
  scale_color_manual(values = colors_55)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Cell_Counts",
    "Before_QC",
    "UMAP_Cell_Counts_by_emulsion_Before_QC_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 23,
  height = 12)
```

There is a batch-dependant repartition, we will have to perform a batch correction later

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Mitochondria {.tabset}

We will use the percentage of mitochondrial reads as a quality control (QC) metric to identify low-quality or potentially dying cells.

::: callout-tip
This metric will be computed using the `perCellQCMetrics()` function from the scater package. Outlier cells will then be detected using the `isOutlier()` function, where cells are flagged as outliers if their mitochondrial percentage deviates by more than 3 times the Median Absolute Deviation (MAD) from the median.
:::

In addition, the distribution of mitochondrial content will be visualized across emulsions to assess potential batch effects or condition-specific differences.

#### Run {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Run Mito}
# !!! Be careful to perform QC on counts slot (raw data) !!!

qc_mito <- perCellQCMetrics(
  seu_CD8@assays$RNA@counts,
  subsets = list(mito = grep("^MT-", rownames(seu_CD8@assays$RNA@counts), value = TRUE)))

seu_CD8 <- AddMetaData(seu_CD8, as.data.frame(qc_mito))


qc_mito2 <- seu_CD8@meta.data %>%
  dplyr::select(emulsion, subsets_mito_percent) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(emulsion) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    high_mito_outliers = as.logical(
      scater::isOutlier(
        subsets_mito_percent,
        nmads = 3,
        type = "higher"
      )
    ),
    mito_status = dplyr::case_when(
      high_mito_outliers ~ "high",
      TRUE ~ "normal"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  filter(!is.na(mito_status))

seu_CD8 <- AddMetaData(seu_CD8, qc_mito2$mito_status, col.name = "mito_status")


```

#### Visualize {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Viz Mito 1}
ggplot(seu_CD8@meta.data, aes(x = subsets_mito_percent, y = emulsion, fill = after_stat(x))) +
  geom_density_ridges_gradient(
    scale = 3,
    rel_min_height = 0.01
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis(
    alpha = 0.9,
    name = "subsets_mito_percent",
    option = "C",
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Mitochondrial content by emulsion",
    x = "Percent mitochondrial reads",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.1, "lines"),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

dir.create(
  file.path(DIR_RESULT, 
            "CD8_Tcells", 
            "QC",
            "Mitochondria",
    "Before_QC"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(DIR_RESULT,
                       "CD8_Tcells",
                       "QC",
                       "Mitochondria",
                       "Before_QC",
                       "Ridgeplot_mitochondrial_content_by_emulsion_before_QC_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 8,
  height = 5)
```

```{r QC CD8 Viz Mito 2}
FeaturePlot(
  seu_CD8,
  features = "subsets_mito_percent",
  reduction = "umap",
  order = TRUE,
  cols = c("lightgrey", "red")
)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(DIR_RESULT,
                       "CD8_Tcells",
                       "QC",
                       "Mitochondria",
                       "Before_QC",
                       "UMAP_mitochondrial_content_before_QC_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 6,
  height = 5)
```

```{r QC CD8 Viz Mito 3}
ggplot(seu_CD8@meta.data, aes(x = emulsion, y = subsets_mito_percent, color = mito_status)) +
  geom_violin(fill = "grey90", color = NA) +
  geom_quasirandom(alpha = 0.5, size = 0.8) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("normal" = "green3", "high" = "red3")
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))


ggsave(
  filename = file.path(DIR_RESULT,
                       "CD8_Tcells",
                       "QC",
                       "Mitochondria",
                       "Before_QC",
                       "ViolinPlot_mito_percent_by_emulsion.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)

```

There are some cells with high mito content in some emulsions. We will remove these cells later

Majority of batch have a similar distribution of mitochondrial content, but some emulsions have a higher percentage of mitochondrial reads, which could indicate a higher proportion of low-quality or dying cells in those batches. We will need to take this into account when we will perform QC and filtering to avoid removing too many cells from these batches. Globally, we can see that the majority of cells have a low percentage of mitochondrial reads (\<10%), which is a good sign of overall data quality.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Ribosomes {.tabset}

We will now examine the percentage of ribosomal reads as a quality control (QC) metric to identify cells with high ribosomal content, which may also indicate low-quality or stressed cells. This metric will be computed using the `perCellQCMetrics()` function from the scater package. Outlier cells will be identified using the `isOutlier()` function, where cells are considered outliers if their ribosomal percentage deviates by more than 3 times the Median Absolute Deviation (MAD) from the median.

In addition, the distribution of ribosomal content will be visualized across emulsions to evaluate potential batch effects or condition-specific differences.

#### Run {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Run Ribo}
# !!! Be careful to perform QC on counts slot (raw data) !!!

qc_ribo <- perCellQCMetrics(
  seu_CD8@assays$RNA@counts,
  subsets = list(
    ribo = grep("^RPL|^RPS", rownames(seu_CD8@assays$RNA@counts), value = TRUE)))

seu_CD8 <- AddMetaData(seu_CD8, as.data.frame(qc_ribo))

qc_ribo2 <- seu_CD8@meta.data %>%
  dplyr::select(emulsion, subsets_ribo_percent) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(emulsion) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    high_ribo_outliers  = as.logical(scater::isOutlier(
      subsets_ribo_percent,
      nmads = 3,
      type = "higher"
    )),
    lower_ribo_outliers = as.logical(scater::isOutlier(
      subsets_ribo_percent,
      nmads = 3,
      type = "lower"
    )),
    ribo_status = dplyr::case_when(
      high_ribo_outliers  ~ "high",
      lower_ribo_outliers ~ "low",
      TRUE ~ "normal"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  filter(!is.na(ribo_status))

seu_CD8 <- AddMetaData(seu_CD8, qc_ribo2$ribo_status, col.name = "ribo_status")

```

#### Visualize {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Viz Ribo 1}
ggplot(seu_CD8@meta.data, aes(x = subsets_ribo_percent, y = emulsion, fill = after_stat(x))) +
  geom_density_ridges_gradient(
    scale = 3,
    rel_min_height = 0.01
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis(
    alpha = 0.9,
    name = "subsets_ribo_percent",
    option = "C",
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Ribosomal content by emulsion",
    x = "Percent ribosomal reads",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.1, "lines"),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

dir.create(
  file.path(DIR_RESULT,
            "CD8_Tcells",
            "QC", 
            "Ribosomal",
            "Before_QC"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(DIR_RESULT,
                       "CD8_Tcells",
                       "QC",
                       "Ribosomal",
                       "Before_QC",
                       "Ridgeplot_ribosomal_content_by_emulsion_before_QC_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 8,
  height = 5)
```

```{r QC CD8 Viz Ribo 2}
FeaturePlot(
  seu_CD8,
  features = "subsets_ribo_percent",
  reduction = "umap",
  order = TRUE,
  cols = c("lightgrey", "red"))

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(DIR_RESULT,
                       "CD8_Tcells",
                       "QC",
                       "Ribosomal",
                       "Before_QC",
                       "UMAP_ribosomal_content_before_QC_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 6,
  height = 5)
```

```{r QC CD8 Viz Ribo 3}
ggplot(seu_CD8@meta.data, aes(x = emulsion, y = subsets_ribo_percent, color = ribo_status)) +
  geom_violin(fill = "grey90", color = NA) +
  geom_quasirandom(alpha = 0.5, size = 0.8) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("normal" = "green3", "high" = "red3", "low" = "blue3")
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))


ggsave(
  filename = file.path(DIR_RESULT,
                       "CD8_Tcells",
                       "QC",
                       "Ribosomal",
                       "Before_QC",
                       "ViolinPlot_ribo_percent_by_emulsion.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

All batch have almost the same distribution of % ribo reads in cells.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Library size {.tabset}

We will now examine library size (nCountRNA) as a quality control (QC) metric to identify cells with unusually low or high total counts, which can be indicative of low-quality or stressed cells. This metric will be computed using the `perCellQCMetrics()` function from the scater package. Outlier cells will be detected using the `isOutlier()` function, where cells are considered outliers if their library size deviates by more than 3 times the Median Absolute Deviation (MAD) from the median.

In addition, the distribution of library size will be visualized across emulsions to evaluate potential batch effects or condition-specific differences.

#### Run {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Run Libsize}
qc_lib_size <- seu_CD8@meta.data %>%
  dplyr::group_by(emulsion) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    low_lib  = as.logical(scater::isOutlier(nCount_RNA, nmads = 3, type = "lower")),
    high_lib = as.logical(scater::isOutlier(nCount_RNA, nmads = 3, type = "higher")),
    libsize_status = dplyr::case_when(
      low_lib  ~ "low",
      high_lib ~ "high",
      TRUE ~ "normal"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  filter(nCount_RNA > 0)

# Add libsize_status to metadata
seu_CD8 <- AddMetaData(seu_CD8, qc_lib_size$libsize_status, col.name = "libsize_status")
```

#### Visualize {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Viz Libsize 1}
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 8


ggplot(qc_lib_size, aes(x = nCount_RNA)) +
  geom_density(fill = "grey80", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_rug(
    aes(color = libsize_status),
    alpha = 0.6,
    sides = "b"
  ) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      low = "blue",
      normal = "green",
      high = "red"
    )
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~emulsion) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    color = "Library size QC",
    x = "nCount_RNA (log10)",
    y = "Density"
  )

dir.create(
  file.path(DIR_RESULT,
            "CD8_Tcells",
            "QC", 
            "Library_Size",
            "Before_QC"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(DIR_RESULT,
                       "CD8_Tcells",
                       "QC",
                       "Library_Size",
                       "Before_QC",
                       "DensityPlot_library_size_density_by_emulsion.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

```{r QC CD8 Viz Libsize 2}
ggplot(qc_lib_size, aes(x = emulsion, y = nCount_RNA, color = libsize_status)) +
  geom_violin(fill = "grey90", color = NA) +
  geom_quasirandom(alpha = 0.5, size = 0.8) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(low = "blue", normal = "green", high = "red")
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))


ggsave(
  filename = file.path(DIR_RESULT,
                       "CD8_Tcells",
                       "QC",
                       "Library_Size",
                       "Before_QC",
                       "ViolinPlot_library_size_by_emulsion.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

We observe that some cells exhibit unusually low or high library sizes in certain emulsions. These cells will be removed or retained later depending on the final QC filtering thresholds and downstream quality control decisions.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Library diversity {.tabset}

We will now examine library diversity (nFeatureRNA) as a quality control (QC) metric to identify cells with unusually low or high gene diversity, which can also indicate low-quality or stressed cells. This metric will be computed using the `perCellQCMetrics()` function from the scater package. Outlier cells will be identified using the `isOutlier()` function, where cells are considered outliers if their library diversity deviates by more than 3 times the Median Absolute Deviation (MAD) from the median.

In addition, the distribution of library diversity will be visualized across emulsions to assess potential batch effects or condition-specific differences.

#### Run {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Run Libdiv}
qc_lib_diversity <- seu_CD8@meta.data %>%
  dplyr::group_by(emulsion) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    low_feat  = as.logical(scater::isOutlier(nFeature_RNA, nmads = 3, type = "lower", log = TRUE)),
    high_feat = as.logical(scater::isOutlier(nFeature_RNA, nmads = 3, type = "higher", log = TRUE)),
    libdiv_status = dplyr::case_when(
      low_feat  ~ "low",
      high_feat ~ "high",
      TRUE ~ "normal"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  filter(nFeature_RNA > 0)

# Add libdiv_status to metadata
seu_CD8 <- AddMetaData(seu_CD8, qc_lib_diversity$libdiv_status, col.name = "libdiv_status")
```

#### Visualize {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Viz Libdiv 1}
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 8


ggplot(qc_lib_diversity, aes(x = nFeature_RNA)) +
  geom_density(fill = "grey80", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_rug(
    aes(color = libdiv_status),
    alpha = 0.6,
    sides = "b"
  ) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      low = "blue",
      normal = "green",
      high = "red"
    )
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~emulsion) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    color = "Library diversity QC",
    x = "nFeatureRNA (log10)",
    y = "Density"
  )

dir.create(
  file.path(DIR_RESULT,
            "CD8_Tcells",
            "QC", 
            "Library_Diversity",
            "Before_QC"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(DIR_RESULT,
                       "CD8_Tcells",
                       "QC",
                       "Library_Diversity",
                       "Before_QC",
                       "DensityPlot_library_diversity_density_by_emulsion.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

```{r QC CD8 Viz Libdiv 2}
ggplot(qc_lib_diversity, aes(x = emulsion, y = nFeature_RNA, color = libdiv_status)) +
  geom_violin(fill = "grey90", color = NA) +
  geom_quasirandom(alpha = 0.5, size = 0.8) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(low = "blue", normal = "green", high = "red")
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(DIR_RESULT,
                       "CD8_Tcells",
                       "QC",
                       "Library_Diversity",
                       "Before_QC",
                       "ViolinPlot_library_diversity_by_emulsion.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

We don't observe cells that exhibit unusually low or high library diversity. These cells will be removed or retained later depending on the final QC filtering thresholds and downstream quality control decisions.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Librairy complexity {.tabset}

We will now see the library complexity (log10GenesPerUMI) as a QC metric to identify cells with low or high library complexity, which can also be a sign of low-quality or stressed cells. We will compute this metric using the `perCellQCMetrics()` function from the scater package, and then we will use the `isOutlier()` function to identify outliers based on this metric. Cells connsidered as Ouliers are cells that have a library complexity + ou - 3 times the MAD (Median Absolute Deviation). We will also visualize the distribution of library complexity by emulsion to see if there are any batch effects or differences between conditions.

#### Run {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Run Libcomplex}
seu_CD8$log10_Complexity <- log10(seu_CD8$nFeature_RNA) / log10(seu_CD8$nCount_RNA)

qc_lib_complexity <- seu_CD8@meta.data %>%
  dplyr::select(emulsion, log10_Complexity) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(emulsion) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    low_complexity  = as.logical(scater::isOutlier(
      log10_Complexity,
      nmads = 3,
      type = "lower"
    )),
    high_complexity = as.logical(scater::isOutlier(
      log10_Complexity,
      nmads = 3,
      type = "higher"
    )),
    libcomplex_status = dplyr::case_when(
      low_complexity  ~ "low",
      high_complexity ~ "high",
      TRUE ~ "normal"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  filter(!is.na(log10_Complexity))
  

low_quantil_complexity <- quantile(seu_CD8$log10_Complexity, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)

# Add libcomplex_status to metadata
seu_CD8 <- AddMetaData(seu_CD8, qc_lib_complexity$libcomplex_status, col.name = "libcomplex_status")
```

#### Visualize {.tabset}

The red line represent the 5% quantile of the library complexity distribution. Cells with a library complexity below this threshold are considered as low complexity outliers. We can see that there are some cells with low library complexity in some emulsions, which could indicate a higher proportion of low-quality or stressed cells in those batches. We will need to take this into account when we will perform QC and filtering to avoid removing too many cells from these batches.

```{r QC CD8 Viz Libcomplex 1}
ggplot(seu_CD8@meta.data, aes(x = log10_Complexity)) +
  geom_density(fill = "lightgreen", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = low_quantil_complexity, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Library complexity (log10GenesPerUMI)",
    subtitle = paste0("Red dashed line = 5% quantile (", round(low_quantil_complexity, 2), ")"),
    x = "log10GenesPerUMI"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

dir.create(
  file.path(DIR_RESULT,
            "CD8_Tcells",
            "QC", 
            "Library_Complexity",
            "Before_QC"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(DIR_RESULT,
                       "CD8_Tcells",
                       "QC",
                       "Library_Complexity",
                       "Before_QC",
                       "DensityPlot_library_complexity_before_QC_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

```{r QC CD8 Viz Libcomplex 2}
# ggplot(seu_CD8@meta.data, aes(x = nCount_RNA, y = nFeature_RNA)) +
#   geom_point(
#     data = subset(seu_CD8@meta.data, !low_complexity_outliers),
#     color = "grey70",
#     alpha = 0.25,
#     size = 0.4
#   ) +
#   geom_point(
#     data = subset(seu_CD8@meta.data, low_complexity_outliers),
#     color = "blue3",
#     alpha = 0.7,
#     size = 0.6
#   ) +
#   geom_smooth(formula = y ~ x, method = "lm", color = "black", linewidth = 0.6) +
#   scale_x_log10() +
#   scale_y_log10() +
#   labs(
#     title = "Library complexity",
#     subtitle = "Genes detected vs UMIs (log–log)",
#     x = "UMIs per cell",
#     y = "Genes per cell"
#   ) +
#   theme_classic(base_size = 11)

ggplot(seu_CD8@meta.data, aes(x = nCount_RNA, y = nFeature_RNA, color = libcomplex_status)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(formula = y ~ x, method = "lm", color = "black", linewidth = 0.6) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("normal" = "grey70", "low" = "blue3", "high" = "red3")) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(
    title = "Library complexity",
    subtitle = "Genes(nFeature)/UMIs(nCount) per cell",
    x = "UMIs per cell",
    y = "Genes per cell",
    color = "Complexity status"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11)


ggsave(
  filename = file.path(DIR_RESULT,
                       "CD8_Tcells",
                       "QC",
                       "Library_Complexity",
                       "Before_QC",
                       "ScatterPlot_library_complexity_before_QC_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

```{r QC CD8 Viz Libcomplex 3}
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 10


ggplot(seu_CD8@meta.data,
       aes(x = log10_Complexity, fill = emulsion)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(
    seu_CD8@meta.data,
    xintercept = median(seu_CD8@meta.data$log10_Complexity) - 3 * mad(seu_CD8@meta.data$log10_Complexity),
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "blue3"
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    seu_CD8@meta.data,
    xintercept = median(seu_CD8@meta.data$log10_Complexity) + 3 * mad(seu_CD8@meta.data$log10_Complexity),
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "red3"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ emulsion, ncol = 4) +
  labs(
    title = "Library complexity by emulsion",
    x = "log10GenesPerUMI"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(DIR_RESULT,
                       "CD8_Tcells",
                       "QC",
                       "Library_Complexity",
                       "Before_QC",
                       "DensityPlot_library_complexity_by_emulsion_before_QC_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

```{r QC CD8 Viz Libcomplex 4}
ggplot(seu_CD8@meta.data,
       aes(x = emulsion, y = log10_Complexity, color = libcomplex_status)) +
  geom_violin(fill = "grey90", color = NA) +
  geom_quasirandom(alpha = 0.4, size = 0.6) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(low = "blue", normal = "green", high = "red")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Library complexity per emulsion",
    y = "log10GenesPerUMI",
    x = NULL
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(DIR_RESULT,
                       "CD8_Tcells",
                       "QC",
                       "Library_Complexity",
                       "Before_QC",
                       "ViolinPlot_library_complexity_by_emulsion_before_QC_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

```{r QC CD8 Viz Libcomplex 5}
ggplot(seu_CD8@meta.data,
       aes(x = log10_Complexity, fill = libcomplex_status)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("high" = "red3", "normal" = "green3", "low" = "blue3")) +
  theme_classic()

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(DIR_RESULT,
                       "CD8_Tcells",
                       "QC",
                       "Library_Complexity",
                       "Before_QC",
                       "DensityPlot_library_complexity_outliers_before_QC_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Cell Cycle {.tabset}

We will now perform cell cycle scoring to identify the cell cycle phase of each cell, which can also be a sign of low-quality or stressed cells. We will use the `CellCycleScoring()` function from the Seurat package, which uses a set of canonical cell cycle genes to assign each cell to a specific cell cycle phase (G1, S, G2M). We will also visualize the distribution of cell cycle phases by emulsion to see if there are any batch effects or differences between conditions.

::: callout-tip
It is important to see if cluster found after dimensionality reduction and clustering are enriched in specific cell cycle phases, which could indicate that the clustering is driven by cell cycle effects rather than biological differences of interest. If we find that certain clusters are predominantly composed of cells in a specific cell cycle phase, we may need to consider regressing out the cell cycle effects or using alternative clustering methods that are less sensitive to cell cycle variation.
:::

#### Run {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Run cellcycle}
seu_CD8 <- CellCycleScoring(
  seu_CD8,
  s.features = cc.genes$s.genes,
  g2m.features = cc.genes$g2m.genes,
  set.ident = FALSE
)
```

#### Visualize {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Viz cellcycle}
DimPlot(seu_CD8, group.by = "Phase") +
  ggtitle("Cell cycle phase before QC CD8 Tcells") +
  facet_wrap(~Phase)

dir.create(
  file.path(DIR_RESULT,
            "CD8_Tcells",
            "QC",
            "Cell_Cycle",
            "Before_QC"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Cell_Cycle",
    "Before_QC",
    "UMAP_Cell_Cycle_Phase_before_QC_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

Later, see if clusters are enriched in specific cell cycle phases. For now with prevoius umap (done on total T cells, not CD8 only) we cannot see a specific regroupement of cells in specific cell cycle phase, but we will see this after dimensionality reduction and clustering on CD8 T cells only.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Filtering {.tabset}

We have look at several QC metrics we will now set a cutoff for those to eliminate bad quality cells from our dataset. We have flag each cells for each QC metric based on the distribution of the data and the visualizations we have done. We have then create a new column in the metadata to indicate whether each cell passes or fails the QC criteria based on these cutoffs. Finally, we will subset the Seurat object to keep only the cells that pass QC for downstream analysis.

::: callout-tip
There are several way to set cutoff... we can do this the easy way… or the hard way ! It’s your choice.

-   Stringent QC will remove all cells that fail at least one QC metric (mito, ribo, libsize, libdiv, libcomplex)

-   Non stringent QC will remove only cells that fail all QC metrics (mito, ribo, libsize, libdiv, libcomplex)

    -   Often all cells pass QC so this is not really a good option

-   Moderate QC will remove cells that fail at least 2 (or 3) QC metrics (mito, ribo, libsize, libdiv, libcomplex) according what we thing it's the best compromise between stringency and cell retention.
:::

For Stringent QC :

```{r QC CD8 Cutoff Stringent QC}
seu_CD8@meta.data$Stringent_QC_status <- dplyr::case_when(
  # seu_CD8$DblFinder_class == "doublet" ~ "fail_doublet",
  (seu_CD8$mito_status == "high" | seu_CD8$libsize_status == "low" | seu_CD8$libdiv_status == "low" | seu_CD8$libcomplex_status == "low") ~ "fail",
  TRUE ~ "pass"
)

# Number of cells that fail QC
table(seu_CD8@meta.data$Stringent_QC_status)

# Add percentage of cells that fail QC for each emulsion and globally

```

On 30269 CD8+ Tcells, 792 cells fail the stringent QC (2.62% of cells).

For Non Stringent QC :

```{r QC CD8 Cutoff Non Stringent QC}
seu_CD8@meta.data$Non_stringent_QC_status <- dplyr::case_when(
  # seu_CD8$DblFinder_class == "doublet" ~ "fail_doublet",
  (seu_CD8$mito_status == "high" & seu_CD8$libsize_status == "low" & seu_CD8$libdiv_status == "low" & seu_CD8$libcomplex_status == "low") ~ "fail",
  TRUE ~ "pass"
)

# Number of cells that fail QC
table(seu_CD8@meta.data$Non_stringent_QC_status)
```

On 30269 CD8+ Tcells, 0 cells fail the non stringent QC (0% of cells).

::: callout-warning
This QC is not really good because it does not remove any cell from the dataset, even if some cells fail one or more QC metrics. It can be useful to keep all cells for some analysis, but it can also introduce noise and bias in the downstream analysis if we have a lot of low-quality cells in the dataset.
:::

I have chose the moderate QC for the downstream analysis.

For Moderate QC :

```{r QC CD8 Cutoff Moderate QC}
seu_CD8@meta.data$Moderate_QC_status <- dplyr::case_when(
  # seu_CD8$DblFinder_class == "doublet" ~ "fail_doublet",
  (seu_CD8$mito_status == "high" | seu_CD8$libcomplex_status == "low") ~ "fail",
  TRUE ~ "pass"
)

# Number of cells that fail QC
table(seu_CD8@meta.data$Moderate_QC_status)
```

With Moderate QC we have 791 cells that failed QC and the rest pass. Hence, we retain 29891 cells for downstream analysis, which represent 97.38% of the original dataset. This QC is a good compromise between stringency and cell retention, as it removes cells that fail at least 2 QC metrics (mito high and libcomplex low) while keeping the majority of cells in the dataset.

We subset dataset to keep only cells that pass QC for downstream analysis.

```{r QC CD8 Cutoff/Subset}
# Subset the Seurat object to keep only cells that pass QC
seu_CD8_QC_Moderate <- subset(seu_CD8, subset = Moderate_QC_status == "pass")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Post-filtering QC {.tabset}

### Cells count {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Viz cell counts After QC 2}
features <- c("condition", "id")

dir.create(
  file.path(DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Cell_Counts", 
    "After_QC"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)


for (feature in features) {
  p <- seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@meta.data %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = .data[[feature]], fill = .data[[feature]])) +
    geom_bar() +
    scale_fill_manual(values = pal, drop = FALSE) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
    ) +
    ggtitle(paste("NCells -", feature)) +
    NoLegend()

  print(p)
  
  ggsave(
  plot = p,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Cell_Counts",
    "After_QC",
    paste0("Cell_Counts_By_", feature, "_After_QC_CD8_Tcells.png")),
  width = 6,
  height = 4)
}


features <- c("emulsion")

for (feature in features) {
  p <- seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@meta.data %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = .data[[feature]], fill = .data[[feature]])) +
    geom_bar() +
    # scale_fill_manual(values = pal2, drop = FALSE) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
    ) +
    ggtitle(paste("NCells -", feature)) +
    NoLegend()

  print(p)
  
  ggsave(
  plot = p,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Cell_Counts",
    "After_QC",
    paste0("Cell_Counts_By_", feature, "_After_QC_CD8_Tcells.png")),
  width = 6,
  height = 4)
}
```

```{r QC CD8 Viz cell counts After QC 3}
#| fig-width: 20
#| fig-height: 20
#| out-width: 100%

DimPlot(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, group.by = "emulsion") +
  facet_wrap(~emulsion, ncol = 6) +
  ggtitle("UMAP by emulsion after QC CD8 Tcells") +
  NoLegend()

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Cell_Counts",
    "After_QC",
    "UMAP_Cell_Counts_by_emulsion_After_QC_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 23,
  height = 12)
```

```{r QC CD8 Viz cell counts After QC 1}
table_interactive <- seu_CD8@meta.data %>%
  count(emulsion, condition, name = "Cells") %>%
  mutate(
    Percent_global = percent(Cells / sum(Cells), accuracy = 0.1) ) %>% 
  group_by(condition) %>%
  mutate(
    Percent_within_condition = percent(Cells / sum(Cells), accuracy = 0.1)
  ) %>%  ungroup() %>%
  rename(Batch = emulsion) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = condition,
    values_from = Percent_within_condition,
    values_fill = "0%"
  ) %>% 
  mutate(
    MM = ifelse(is.na(MM), "0%", MM),
    HD = ifelse(is.na(HD), "0%", HD),
    Total = rowSums(select(., contains("Cells")))
  ) %>%
  bind_rows(
    summarise(
      .,
      across(where(is.numeric), sum),
      Batch = "Total"
    )
  )

datatable(
  table_interactive,
  options = list(
    pageLength = 10,
    autoWidth = TRUE
  ),
  rownames = FALSE
)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Mitochondria {.tabset}

#### Run {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Run Mito After QC}
# !!! Be careful to perform QC on counts slot (raw data) !!!

qc_mito <- perCellQCMetrics(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@assays$RNA@counts,
  subsets = list(mito = grep("^MT-", rownames(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@assays$RNA@counts), value = TRUE)))

seu_CD8_QC_Moderate <- AddMetaData(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, as.data.frame(qc_mito))

qc_mito2 <- seu_CD8@meta.data %>%
  dplyr::select(emulsion, subsets_mito_percent) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(emulsion) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    high_mito_outliers = as.logical(
      scater::isOutlier(
        subsets_mito_percent,
        nmads = 3,
        type = "higher"
      )
    ),
    mito_status = dplyr::case_when(
      high_mito_outliers ~ "high",
      TRUE ~ "normal"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  filter(!is.na(mito_status))

seu_CD8_QC_Moderate <- AddMetaData(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, qc_mito2$mito_status, col.name = "mito_status")


```

#### Visualize {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Viz Mito After QC 1}
ggplot(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@meta.data, aes(x = subsets_mito_percent, y = emulsion, fill = after_stat(x))) +
  geom_density_ridges_gradient(
    scale = 3,
    rel_min_height = 0.01
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis(
    alpha = 0.9,
    name = "subsets_mito_percent",
    option = "C",
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Mitochondrial content by emulsion",
    x = "Percent mitochondrial reads",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.1, "lines"),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

dir.create(
  file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC", 
    "Mitochondria",
    "After_QC"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Mitochondria",
    "After_QC", "Ridgeplot_mitochondrial_content_by_emulsion_after_QC_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 8,
  height = 5)
```

```{r QC CD8 Viz Mito After QC 2}
FeaturePlot(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate,
  features = "subsets_mito_percent",
  reduction = "umap",
  order = TRUE,
  cols = c("lightgrey", "red")
)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Mitochondria",
    "After_QC", "UMAP_mitochondrial_content_after_QC_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 6,
  height = 5)
```

```{r QC CD8 Viz Mito After QC 3}
ggplot(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@meta.data, aes(x = emulsion, y = subsets_mito_percent, color = mito_status)) +
  geom_violin(fill = "grey90", color = NA) +
  geom_quasirandom(alpha = 0.5, size = 0.8) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("normal" = "green3", "high" = "red3")
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))


ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Mitochondria",
    "After_QC",
    "ViolinPlot_after_QC_mito_percent_by_emulsion.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)

```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Ribosomes {.tabset}

#### Run {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Run Ribo After QC}
# !!! Be careful to perform QC on counts slot (raw data) !!!

qc_ribo <- perCellQCMetrics(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@assays$RNA@counts,
  subsets = list(
    ribo = grep("^RPL|^RPS", rownames(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@assays$RNA@counts), value = TRUE)))

seu_CD8_QC_Moderate <- AddMetaData(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, as.data.frame(qc_ribo))

qc_ribo2 <- seu_CD8@meta.data %>%
  dplyr::select(emulsion, subsets_ribo_percent) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(emulsion) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    high_ribo_outliers  = as.logical(scater::isOutlier(
      subsets_ribo_percent,
      nmads = 3,
      type = "higher"
    )),
    lower_ribo_outliers = as.logical(scater::isOutlier(
      subsets_ribo_percent,
      nmads = 3,
      type = "lower"
    )),
    ribo_status = dplyr::case_when(
      high_ribo_outliers  ~ "high",
      lower_ribo_outliers ~ "low",
      TRUE ~ "normal"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  filter(!is.na(ribo_status))

seu_CD8_QC_Moderate <- AddMetaData(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, qc_ribo2$ribo_status, col.name = "ribo_status")

```

#### Visualize {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Viz Ribo After QC 1}
ggplot(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@meta.data, aes(x = subsets_ribo_percent, y = emulsion, fill = after_stat(x))) +
  geom_density_ridges_gradient(
    scale = 3,
    rel_min_height = 0.01
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis(
    alpha = 0.9,
    name = "subsets_ribo_percent",
    option = "C",
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Ribosomal content by emulsion",
    x = "Percent ribosomal reads",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.1, "lines"),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

dir.create(
  file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Ribosomal",
    "After_QC"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Ribosomal",
    "After_QC", 
    "Ridgeplot_ribosomal_content_by_emulsion_after_QC_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 8,
  height = 5)
```

```{r QC CD8 Viz Ribo After QC 2}
FeaturePlot(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate,
  features = "subsets_ribo_percent",
  reduction = "umap",
  order = TRUE,
  cols = c("lightgrey", "red"))

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Ribosomal",
    "After_QC",
    "UMAP_ribosomal_content_after_QC_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 6,
  height = 5)
```

```{r QC CD8 Viz Ribo After QC 3}
ggplot(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@meta.data, aes(x = emulsion, y = subsets_ribo_percent, color = ribo_status)) +
  geom_violin(fill = "grey90", color = NA) +
  geom_quasirandom(alpha = 0.5, size = 0.8) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("normal" = "green3", "high" = "red3", "low" = "blue3")
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))


ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Ribosomal",
    "After_QC",
    "ViolinPlot_ribo_percent_by_emulsion_after_QC.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Library size {.tabset}

#### Run {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Run Libsize After QC}
qc_lib_size <- seu_CD8@meta.data %>%
  dplyr::group_by(emulsion) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    low_lib  = as.logical(scater::isOutlier(nCount_RNA, nmads = 3, type = "lower")),
    high_lib = as.logical(scater::isOutlier(nCount_RNA, nmads = 3, type = "higher")),
    libsize_status = dplyr::case_when(
      low_lib  ~ "low",
      high_lib ~ "high",
      TRUE ~ "normal"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  filter(nCount_RNA > 0)

# Add libsize_status to metadata
seu_CD8_QC_Moderate <- AddMetaData(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, qc_lib_size$libsize_status, col.name = "libsize_status")
```

#### Visualize {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Viz Libsize After QC1}
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 8


ggplot(qc_lib_size, aes(x = nCount_RNA)) +
  geom_density(fill = "grey80", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_rug(
    aes(color = libsize_status),
    alpha = 0.6,
    sides = "b"
  ) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      low = "blue",
      normal = "green",
      high = "red"
    )
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~emulsion) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    color = "Library size QC",
    x = "nCount_RNA (log10)",
    y = "Density"
  )

dir.create(
  file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC", 
    "Library_Size",
    "After_QC"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Library_Size",
    "After_QC",
    "DensityPlot_library_size_density_by_emulsion_after_QC.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

```{r QC CD8 Viz Libsize After QC 2}
ggplot(qc_lib_size, aes(x = emulsion, y = nCount_RNA, color = libsize_status)) +
  geom_violin(fill = "grey90", color = NA) +
  geom_quasirandom(alpha = 0.5, size = 0.8) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(low = "blue", normal = "green", high = "red")
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))


ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Library_Size",
    "After_QC",
    "ViolinPlot_library_size_by_emulsion_after_QC.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Library diversity {.tabset}

#### Run {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Run Libdiv After QC}
qc_lib_diversity <- seu_CD8@meta.data %>%
  dplyr::group_by(emulsion) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    low_feat  = as.logical(scater::isOutlier(nFeature_RNA, nmads = 3, type = "lower", log = TRUE)),
    high_feat = as.logical(scater::isOutlier(nFeature_RNA, nmads = 3, type = "higher", log = TRUE)),
    libdiv_status = dplyr::case_when(
      low_feat  ~ "low",
      high_feat ~ "high",
      TRUE ~ "normal"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  filter(nFeature_RNA > 0)

# Add libdiv_status to metadata
seu_CD8_QC_Moderate <- AddMetaData(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, qc_lib_diversity$libdiv_status, col.name = "libdiv_status")
```

#### Visualize {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Viz Libdiv After QC 1}
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 8


ggplot(qc_lib_diversity, aes(x = nFeature_RNA)) +
  geom_density(fill = "grey80", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_rug(
    aes(color = libdiv_status),
    alpha = 0.6,
    sides = "b"
  ) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      low = "blue",
      normal = "green",
      high = "red"
    )
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~emulsion) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    color = "Library diversity QC",
    x = "nFeatureRNA (log10)",
    y = "Density"
  )

dir.create(
  file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC", 
    "Library_Diversity",
    "After_QC"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Library_Diversity",
    "After_QC", 
    "DensityPlot_library_diversity_density_by_emulsion_after_QC.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

```{r QC CD8 Viz Libdiv After QC 2}
ggplot(qc_lib_diversity, aes(x = emulsion, y = nFeature_RNA, color = libdiv_status)) +
  geom_violin(fill = "grey90", color = NA) +
  geom_quasirandom(alpha = 0.5, size = 0.8) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(low = "blue", normal = "green", high = "red")
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Library_Diversity",
    "After_QC",
    "ViolinPlot_library_diversity_by_emulsion_after_QC.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Librairy complexity {.tabset}

#### Run {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Run Libcomplex After QC}
seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$log10_Complexity <- log10(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$nFeature_RNA) / log10(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$nCount_RNA)

qqc_lib_complexity <- seu_CD8@meta.data %>%
  dplyr::select(emulsion, log10_Complexity) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(emulsion) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    low_complexity  = as.logical(scater::isOutlier(
      log10_Complexity,
      nmads = 3,
      type = "lower"
    )),
    high_complexity = as.logical(scater::isOutlier(
      log10_Complexity,
      nmads = 3,
      type = "higher"
    )),
    libcomplex_status = dplyr::case_when(
      low_complexity  ~ "low",
      high_complexity ~ "high",
      TRUE ~ "normal"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  filter(!is.na(log10_Complexity))

low_quantil_complexity <- quantile(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$log10_Complexity, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)

# Add libcomplex_status to metadata
seu_CD8_QC_Moderate <- AddMetaData(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, qc_lib_complexity$libcomplex_status, col.name = "libcomplex_status")
```

#### Visualize {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Viz Libcomplex 1 after QC}
ggplot(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@meta.data, aes(x = log10_Complexity)) +
  geom_density(fill = "lightgreen", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = low_quantil_complexity, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Library complexity (log10GenesPerUMI)",
    subtitle = paste0("Red dashed line = 5% quantile (", round(low_quantil_complexity, 2), ")"),
    x = "log10GenesPerUMI"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

dir.create(
  file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC", 
    "Library_Complexity",
    "After_QC"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Library_Complexity",
    "After_QC", 
    "DensityPlot_library_complexity_after_QC_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

```{r QC CD8 Viz Libcomplex After QC 2}
# ggplot(seu_CD8@meta.data, aes(x = nCount_RNA, y = nFeature_RNA)) +
#   geom_point(
#     data = subset(seu_CD8@meta.data, !low_complexity_outliers),
#     color = "grey70",
#     alpha = 0.25,
#     size = 0.4
#   ) +
#   geom_point(
#     data = subset(seu_CD8@meta.data, low_complexity_outliers),
#     color = "blue3",
#     alpha = 0.7,
#     size = 0.6
#   ) +
#   geom_smooth(formula = y ~ x, method = "lm", color = "black", linewidth = 0.6) +
#   scale_x_log10() +
#   scale_y_log10() +
#   labs(
#     title = "Library complexity",
#     subtitle = "Genes detected vs UMIs (log–log)",
#     x = "UMIs per cell",
#     y = "Genes per cell"
#   ) +
#   theme_classic(base_size = 11)

ggplot(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@meta.data, aes(x = nCount_RNA, y = nFeature_RNA, color = libcomplex_status)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(formula = y ~ x, method = "lm", color = "black", linewidth = 0.6) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("normal" = "grey70", "low" = "blue3", "high" = "red3")) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(
    title = "Library complexity",
    subtitle = "Genes(nFeature)/UMIs(nCount) per cell",
    x = "UMIs per cell",
    y = "Genes per cell",
    color = "Complexity status"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11)


ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Library_Complexity",
    "After_QC", 
    "ScatterPlot_library_complexity_after_QC_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

```{r QC CD8 Viz Libcomplex After QC 3}
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 10


ggplot(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@meta.data,
       aes(x = log10_Complexity, fill = emulsion)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(
    seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@meta.data,
    xintercept = median(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@meta.data$log10_Complexity) - 3 * mad(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@meta.data$log10_Complexity),
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "blue3"
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@meta.data,
    xintercept = median(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@meta.data$log10_Complexity) + 3 * mad(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@meta.data$log10_Complexity),
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "red3"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ emulsion, ncol = 4) +
  labs(
    title = "Library complexity by emulsion",
    x = "log10GenesPerUMI"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Library_Complexity",
    "After_QC", 
    "DensityPlot_library_complexity_by_emulsion_after_QC_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

```{r QC CD8 Viz Libcomplex After QC 4}
ggplot(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@meta.data,
       aes(x = emulsion, y = log10_Complexity, color = libcomplex_status)) +
  geom_violin(fill = "grey90", color = NA) +
  geom_quasirandom(alpha = 0.4, size = 0.6) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(low = "blue", normal = "green", high = "red")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Library complexity per emulsion",
    y = "log10GenesPerUMI",
    x = NULL
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Library_Complexity",
    "After_QC", 
    "ViolinPlot_library_complexity_by_emulsion_after_QC_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

```{r QC CD8 Viz Libcomplex After QC 5}
ggplot(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@meta.data,
       aes(x = log10_Complexity, fill = libcomplex_status)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("high" = "red3", "normal" = "green3", "low" = "blue3")) +
  theme_classic()

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Library_Complexity",
    "After_QC", 
    "DensityPlot_library_complexity_outliers_after_QC_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Cell Cycle {.tabset}

#### Run {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Run cellcycle After QC}
seu_CD8_QC_Moderate <- CellCycleScoring(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate,
  s.features = cc.genes$s.genes,
  g2m.features = cc.genes$g2m.genes,
  set.ident = FALSE
)
```

#### Visualize {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Viz cellcycle After QC}
DimPlot(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, group.by = "Phase") +
  ggtitle("Cell cycle phase before QC CD8 Tcells") +
  facet_wrap(~Phase)

dir.create(
  file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Cell_Cycle",
    "After_QC"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Cell_Cycle",
    "After_QC",
    "UMAP_Cell_Cycle_Phase_before_QC_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Doublet detection {.tabset}

Now that we have clean the dataset from low-quality cells based on mitochondrial content, ribosomal content, library size, library diversity, and library complexity, we can proceed to identify and remove potential doublets from our CD8 T cell dataset. Doublets are artifacts that occur when two or more cells are captured together during the single-cell sequencing process, leading to mixed gene expression profiles that can confound downstream analyses.

::: calout-tip
We have to perform this step after removing low-quality cells because the presence of low-quality cells can interfere with the accuracy of doublet detection algorithms, which often rely on gene expression patterns to identify doublets. By first filtering out low-quality cells, we can improve the performance of doublet detection and ensure that we are identifying true doublets rather than artifacts caused by poor-quality data.
:::

The function will attribute a doublet score for each cells and a doublet classification (singlet or doublet) based on the score and a threshold determined by the algorithm.

### Run {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Run DblFinder, warning=FALSE}
sce <- as.SingleCellExperiment(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, assay = "RNA")
sce <- scDblFinder(sce, 
                   samples = colData(sce)$emulsion,
                   verbose = FALSE)

seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$doublet_score <- sce$scDblFinder.score
seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$doublet_class <- sce$scDblFinder.class
```

We cann see that it is heterogenous between emulsions, with some emulsions having higher doublet scores and more doublets than others. This could be due to technical variations during the sample preparation or sequencing process, or it could reflect biological differences between the samples. It is important to carefully evaluate the doublet scores and classifications, and to consider the potential impact of doublets on downstream analyses, such as clustering and differential expression analysis.

### Visualize before remove {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Viz DblFinder Violin}
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 6
#| fig-cap: "Violin plot of doublet scores by batch before doublet removal"

VlnPlot(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate,
  features = "doublet_score",
  group.by = "emulsion",
  pt.size = 0) +
labs(
  title = "Doublet score by batch",
  x = "batch",
  y = "Doublet score") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal, drop = FALSE) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  NoLegend()

dir.create(
  file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC", 
    "Doublet"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Doublet",
    "ViolinPlot_Doublet_Score_by_emulsion_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)

```

```{r QC CD8 Viz DblFinder Dimplot}
DimPlot(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate,
  group.by = "doublet_class",
  cols = c("singlet" = "grey70", "doublet" = "red3")
)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Doublet",
    "UMAP_Doublet_class_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 6,
  height = 5)
```

### Remove doublets {.tabset}

```{r}
seu_CD8_QC_Moderate <- subset(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, subset = doublet_class == "singlet")

message <- sprintf(
  "Number of cells before doublet removal: %d | Number of cells classified as doublets: %d | Number of cells after doublet removal: %d",
  ncol(sce),
  sum(sce$scDblFinder.class == "doublet"),
  ncol(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate)
)

print(message)
```

We remove those doublets from the dataset to avoid confounding downstream analyses. We can subset the Seurat object to keep only cells classified as "singlet" based on the doublet classification provided by scDblFinder.

Here we remove 865 doublets, which represent 2.8% of the total cells (865 doublets out of 30,000 cells before doublet removal).

::: callout-note
This percentage is within the expected range for doublet rates in single-cell RNA-seq experiments, which typically range from 1% to 10%, depending on the cell loading density and the specific platform used for sequencing. Removing these doublets will help improve the accuracy of downstream analyses, such as clustering and differential expression analysis, by ensuring that we are working with true single-cell data rather than mixed profiles caused by doublets.
:::

### Visualize after remove {.tabset}

```{r QC CD8 Viz DblFinder Violin after remove}
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 6
#| fig-cap: "Violin plot of doublet scores by batch before doublet removal"

VlnPlot(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate,
  features = "doublet_score",
  group.by = "emulsion",
  pt.size = 0
) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal, drop = FALSE) +
labs(
  title = "Doublet score by batch",
  x = "batch",
  y = "Doublet score"
) +
theme_classic() +
theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
NoLegend()

dir.create(
  file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC", 
    "Doublet",
    "After_QC"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Doublet",
    "After_QC",
    "ViolinPlot_Doublet_Score_by_emulsion_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)

```

```{r QC CD8 Viz DblFinder Dimplot after remove}
DimPlot(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate,
  group.by = "doublet_class",
  cols = c("singlet" = "grey70", "doublet" = "red3")
)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "QC",
    "Doublet",
    "After_QC",
    "UMAP_Doublet_class_CD8_Tcells.png"),
  width = 6,
  height = 5)
```

No doublets left in the dataset !

<br>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<br>

# Normalization {.tabset}

Now that we have a clean dataset of CD8 T cells, we can proceed to the normalization step.

::: callout-note
Normalization is a crucial step in single-cell RNA-seq data analysis, as it helps to correct for technical variations and allows for meaningful comparisons of gene expression levels across cells. We will perform normalization separately for the RNA and ADT matrices, as they represent different types of data (gene expression vs protein expression) and may require different normalization methods.
:::

## RNA matrix {.tabset}

For the RNA matrix, we will use the SCTransform method for normalization.

::: callout-note
SCTransform is a variance-stabilizing transformation that models gene expression using a regularized negative binomial regression. It accounts for technical noise and allows for more accurate identification of variable features, which can improve downstream analyses such as PCA, UMAP, and clustering. + We can regress some vars, like here mito percent per cells, it means that we remove the effect of this variable for calculation of normalisation factors and variance stabilization, which can help to reduce the influence of mitochondrial gene expression on downstream analyses, as it is often a source of technical variation in scRNA-seq data. By using SCTransform, we can ensure that our normalization is robust and that we are focusing on the most biologically relevant features for our analysis.

We also set the number of variable features to 3000, which is a common choice for scRNA-seq data and can be optimized based on the specific dataset and downstream analyses. By tuning this parameter, we can find the optimal number of variable features that capture the most informative genes for our analysis while avoiding noise from less informative genes.
:::

::: callout-warning
Optimize and tune according PCA, UMAP, clustering results the variable.feature arguments. Know how to justify 3000 variable features...
:::

```{r CD8 Normalize RNA}
DefaultAssay(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate) <- "RNA"

options(future.globals.maxSize = Inf)
future::plan("sequential")

seu_CD8_QC_Moderate <- SCTransform(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate,
  vars.to.regress = c("subsets_mito_percent"),  # optionnal 
  variable.features.n = 3000, # To optimize and tune according PCA, UMAP, clustering results (see later)
  vst.flavor = "v2",
  verbose = TRUE)
```

::: callout-tip
By regressing out the mitochondrial percentage, we can reduce the influence of mitochondrial gene expression on downstream analyses, which is often a source of technical variation in scRNA-seq data.
:::

::: callout-tip
Don't hesitate to test ***different parameters*** (e.g., ***variable.features.n***) and evaluate their impact on downstream analyses (PCA, UMAP, clustering) to find the optimal settings for your dataset.
:::

## ADT matrix {.tabset}

For the ADT matrix, we will use the CLR (Centered Log-Ratio) normalization method.

::: callout-note
It is commonly used for CITE-seq data. CLR normalization divides each count by the geometric mean of the values in that cell, then log-transforms the result. This method helps to account for the multiplicative nature of technical variations in ADT data, such as differences in antibody capture efficiency and background noise.
:::

```{r CD8 Normalize ADT}
DefaultAssay(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate) <- "ADT"

seu_CD8_QC_Moderate <- NormalizeData(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, 
  normalization.method = "CLR", # CLR = Centered Log-Ratio : standard for ADT (CITE-seq) data (Divide/Center each count by the geometric mean of the values in that cell, then log-transform)
  margin = 2, # Normalize across cells (so cols)
  assay = "ADT")

# Les counts ADT sont dominés par des rapports :
# efficacité de l’anticorps
# accessibilité de l’épitope
# capture / lavage
# bruit de fond
# 
# 👉 Si une cellule capte “2× plus d’anticorps”, toutes les protéines montent proportionnellement.
# 
# C’est un effet multiplicatif, pas une addition constante.
```

<br>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<br>

# Most Variable Features {.tabset}

::: callout-note
We don't need it here because SCTTransform already identifies variable features during the normalization step and stores them in the "SCT" assay. However, if we were to use a different normalization method (e.g., NormalizeData), we would need to run FindVariableFeatures on the "RNA" assay to identify the most variable genes for downstream analyses.
:::

## RNA matrix {.tabset}

```{r CD8 MVP RNA}
# DefaultAssay(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate) <- "RNA"
# seu_CD8_QC_Moderate <- FindVariableFeatures(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, 
#                             assay = "RNA",
#                             selection.method = "vst",
#                             nfeatures = 2000)
```

::: callout-note
Not nécessary as we used SCTransform for normalization, which already identifies variable features during the process and stores them in the "SCT" assay. Otherwise, run NormalizeData and then FindVariableFeatures (with specific argument/options) on the "RNA" assay.
:::

::: callout-note
SCTTransform is based on Binomial Neg and perform Pearson residuals to identify variable features, which is more robust and accurate than traditional methods (e.g., vst) for scRNA-seq data. It accounts for technical noise and allows for better identification of biologically relevant features, leading to improved downstream analyses (PCA, UMAP, clustering).
