```{r CD8 Volcano plot Top10}
      title = paste("Volcano plot – Cluster", i),
      subtitle = "DE genes vs rest",
      x = "avg log2FC",
      y = "-log10(adj p-value)",
      color = "Regulation"
    ) +

    theme_classic(base_size = 12)
  
  print(p)

  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(
      DIR_RESULT,
      "CD8_Tcells",
      "Annotation",
      "Volcano_Plots",
      paste0("Volcano_Cluster_", i, ".png")
    ),
    plot = p,
    width = 8,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )
}

```


```{r Enhanced Volcano plot Top10}
# library(EnhancedVolcano)
# EnhancedVolcano(
#   markers,
#   lab = markers$gene,
#   x = 'avg_log2FC',
#   y = 'p_val_adj',
#   pCutoff = 0.05,
#   FCcutoff = 0.5,
#   pointSize = 2,
#   labSize = 3,
#   title = paste("Cluster", i)
# )
```
::::

## Automatic annotation {.tabset}

::: callout-note
We use an automatic annotations of clusters based on the SingleR package, which compares the gene expression profiles of our clusters to reference datasets of known cell types to assign putative identities to each cluster. This approach can help us identify the likely cell type or subpopulation represented by each cluster, providing insights into their potential biological functions and relevance in the context of our CD8 T cell dataset. By leveraging SingleR for automatic annotation, we can gain a deeper understanding of the cellular heterogeneity within our dataset and generate hypotheses about the roles of different CD8 T cell subpopulations in health and disease.
:::

### Make automatic predictions {.tabset}

```{r CD8 Run auto annot (SingleR)}
# Prepare SingleR input
sce <- as.SingleCellExperiment(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, assay = "RNA")

# Reference dataset
ref <- fetchReference("monaco_immune", "2024-02-26")

pred_l2 <- SingleR(
  test = sce,
  ref = ref,
  # cluster = seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$seurat_clusters,
  labels = ref$label.main)

pred_l3 <- SingleR(
  test = sce,
  ref = ref,
  # cluster = seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$seurat_clusters,
  labels = ref$label.fine)



seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$predicted.celltype.l2_CD8_jordan <- pred_l2$pruned.labels
seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$predicted.celltype.l3_CD8_jordan <- pred_l3$pruned.labels
```

### Visualization of automatic annotations {.tabset}

```{r CD8 Viz auto Annot l2}
dir.create(
  file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "Annotation",
    "Automatic"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

p1 <- DimPlot(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, 
  reduction = "umap", 
  group.by = "predicted.celltype.l2_CD8_jordan") + 
  # ggtitle("predicted.celltype.l2 for CD8 T cells (SingleR - Monaco reference)") +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal)

p1

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "Annotation",
    "Automatic",
    "UMAP_predicted_celltype_l2_CD8_jordan.png"),
  plot = p1,
  width = 12,
  height = 6)


p2 <- DimPlot(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, 
  reduction = "umap", 
  group.by = "predicted.celltype.l2_CD8_jordan",
  split.by = "predicted.celltype.l2_CD8_jordan") + 
  scale_color_manual(values = pal) +
  NoLegend()
p2

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "Annotation",
    "Automatic",
    "UMAP_predicted_celltype_l2_CD8_jordan_facet.png"),
  plot = p2,
  width = 15,
  height = 10)

p3 <- p1 | p2
# p3

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "Annotation",
    "Automatic",
    "UMAP_predicted_celltype_l2_CD8_jordan_combined.png"),
  plot = p3,
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

```{r CD8 Viz auto Annot l3}
dir.create(
  file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "Annotation",
    "Automatic"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

p1 <- DimPlot(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, 
  reduction = "umap", 
  group.by = "predicted.celltype.l3_CD8_jordan") + 
  # ggtitle("predicted.celltype.l3 for CD8 T cells (SingleR - Monaco reference)") +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal)

p1

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "Annotation",
    "Automatic",
    "UMAP_predicted_celltype_l3_CD8_jordan.png"),
  plot = p1,
  width = 15,
  height = 10)


p2 <- DimPlot(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, 
  reduction = "umap", 
  group.by = "predicted.celltype.l3_CD8_jordan") + 
  scale_color_manual(values = pal) +
  # ggtitle("Wrap predicted.celltype.l3 for CD8 T cells (SingleR - Monaco reference)") +
  facet_wrap(~predicted.celltype.l3_CD8_jordan, ncol = round(length(unique(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$predicted.celltype.l3_CD8_jordan))/2)) + 
  NoLegend()
p2

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "Annotation",
    "Automatic",
    "UMAP_predicted_celltype_l3_CD8_jordan_facet.png"),
  plot = p2,
  width = 15,
  height = 10)

p3 <- p1 | p2
# p3

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "Annotation",
    "Automatic",
    "UMAP_predicted_celltype_l3_CD8_jordan_combined.png"),
  plot = p3,
  width = 15,
  height = 10)
```

```{r}
ct <- table( cluster = seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$seurat_clusters, celltype_l3 = seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$predicted.celltype.l3_CD8_jordan )


ct_prop <- prop.table(ct, margin = 1) %>%
  as.data.frame()

ct_prop_wide <- ct_prop %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = celltype_l3,
    values_from = Freq
  )

ct_prop_wide_fmt <- ct_prop_wide %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x * 100, 1)))

datatable(
  ct_prop_wide_fmt,
  options = list(pageLength = 20, scrollX = TRUE),
  rownames = FALSE,
  caption = "Cluster Composition (%)"
)
```

```{r CD8 Prop by celltype in condition Viz}
props <- seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@meta.data %>%
  count(condition, predicted.celltype.l3_CD8_jordan) %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n))

ggplot(props,
       aes(x = condition,
           y = prop,
           fill = predicted.celltype.l3_CD8_jordan)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", position = "stack")

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "Annotation",
    "Automatic",
    "Proportion_celltype_l3_CD8_jordan_by_condition.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)
```

There is no clear enrichment of specific cell types in one condition or the other, but we can see that some cell types are more abundant than others in our dataset.

In this CD8 T cell dataset, automatic annotation found some cells that are CD4 T cells, which is not expected as we should only have CD8 T cells. That show that automatic annotation may sometimes not be perfect and can lead to misclassification of cell types.

## Manual annotation {.tabset}

In addition of automatic annotation, I can try to make my own manual annotation of clusters based on the expression of known marker genes for CD8 T cell subpopulations. I can use the FeaturePlot function to visualize the expression of these marker genes on the UMAP plot and see if they are enriched in specific clusters. I can also look at the average expression of these marker genes in each cluster to further characterize their identity and function.

### Create my celltype signatures {.tabset}

#### Run presto to find markers {.tabset}

```{r CD8 Run presto}
expr_matrix <- as.matrix(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@assays$RNA@data)
labels <- seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$seurat_clusters

presto_markers <- wilcoxauc(expr_matrix, labels)
```

#### Construction of the signature table {.tabset}

::: callout-tip
I will update signatures by adding or removing genes from a signature. And why not adding some signatures if I find interesting ones in the literature or in other data. I plan to add a NK signature (work in progress)
:::

```{r CD8 Construct signature tibble}
signature_tbl <- tribble(
  ~feature, ~cell_type, ~class,
  
  # Activated
  "CD69", "Activated", "State",
  "TNFRSF9", "Activated", "State",
  "IL2RA", "Activated", "State",

  # Apoptotic / stress
  "MALAT1", "Apoptotic", "State",
  "NEAT1", "Apoptotic", "State",

  # CD4 lineage
  "CD3D", "CD4", "Identity",
  "CD3E", "CD4", "Identity",
  "CD4",  "CD4", "Identity",
  "TRBC1","CD4", "Identity",

  # CD8 lineage
  "FCGR3A","CD8", "Identity",
  "CD3D", "CD8", "Identity",
  "CD3E", "CD8", "Identity",
  "CD8A", "CD8", "Identity",
  "CD8B", "CD8", "Identity",
  "TRBC1","CD8", "Identity",
  "NCR1", "CD8", "Identity",

  # Cytotoxic
  "PRF1","Cytotoxic", "Function",
  "GZMB","Cytotoxic", "Function",
  "GNLY","Cytotoxic", "Function",
  "GZMH","Cytotoxic", "Function",
  "CCL5","Cytotoxic", "Function",
  "NKG7","Cytotoxic", "Function",
  "KLRG1","Cytotoxic", "Function",
  "GZMA","Cytotoxic", "Function",

  # Exhausted
  "PDCD1","Exhausted", "State",
  "LAG3","Exhausted", "State",
  "TOX","Exhausted", "State",
  "TIGIT","Exhausted", "State",
  "CD226","Exhausted", "State",
  "GZMK","Exhausted", "State",
  "HAVCR2","Exhausted", "State",
  "XCL1","Exhausted", "State",
  "XCL2","Exhausted", "State",
  "CD160","Exhausted", "State",
  "ENTPD1","Exhausted", "State",
  "CTLA4","Exhausted", "State",
  "LAYN","Exhausted", "State",
  "LILRB1","Exhausted", "State",
  "CD38","Exhausted", "State",

  # HLA
  "HLA-DRA","HLA", "Function",
  "HLA-DRB1","HLA", "Function",

  # IFN / ISG
  "IFNG","IFN", "Function",
  "IFIT2","IFN", "Function",
  "IFIT3","IFN", "Function",
  "ISG15","IFN", "Function",
  "STAT1","IFN", "Function",
  "IFI6","IFN", "Function",
  "IRF7","IFN", "Function",
  "MX1","IFN", "Function",
  "IFITM3","IFN", "Function",

  # Inflammatory / NFkB
  "TNFAIP3","Inflammatory", "Function",
  "NFKB1","Inflammatory", "Function",
  "NFKBIA","Inflammatory", "Function",
  "NFKBIZ","Inflammatory", "Function",
  "STAT4","Inflammatory", "Function",

  # Memory
  "CD44","Memory", "State",
  "PTPRC","Memory", "State",
  "CCR7","Memory", "State",
  "CD27","Memory", "State",
  "CD28","Memory", "State",

  # Naive
  "CCR7","Naif", "State",
  "SELL","Naif", "State",
  "CD27","Naif", "State",
  "CD28","Naif", "State",
  "TCF7","Naif", "State",
  "IL7R","Naif", "State",
  "LEF1","Naif", "State",

  # Proliferating
  "CENPV","Prolif", "State",
  "MKI67","Prolif", "State",
  "NCAPG","Prolif", "State",
  "KIFC1","Prolif", "State",
  "CENPE","Prolif", "State",
  "ORC6","Prolif", "State",

  # Resident (Trm)
  "CD69","Resident", "State",
  "CXCR6","Resident", "State",
  "NR4A2","Resident", "State",
  "RUNX3","Resident", "State",

  # Senescent
  "KLRG1","Senescent", "State",
  "B3GAT1","Senescent", "State",
  "CD27","Senescent", "State",
  "CD28","Senescent", "State",
  "LAMP1","Senescent", "State",
  "IL1A","Senescent", "State",
  "FAS","Senescent", "State",
  "TP53","Senescent", "State",
  "TP53BP1","Senescent", "State",
  "CDKN1A","Senescent", "State",
  "CDKN2A","Senescent", "State",
  # "MKI67","Senescent", "State",

  # Tfh
  "IL21","Tfh", "Identity",
  # "FR4","Tfh", "Identity", # Only in mouse
  "CXCR5","Tfh", "Identity",
  "BCL6","Tfh", "Identity",
  "PDCD1","Tfh", "Identity",
  "ICOS","Tfh", "Identity",

  # Th1
  "CX3CR1","Th1", "Identity",
  "IFNG","Th1", "Identity",
  "TBX21","Th1", "Identity",
  "CXCR3","Th1", "Identity",
  "STAT4","Th1", "Identity",
  "IL12RB2","Th1", "Identity",

  # Th2
  "GATA3","Th2", "Identity",
  "CCR4","Th2", "Identity",
  "IL4","Th2", "Identity",
  "IL5","Th2", "Identity",
  "IL13","Th2", "Identity",

  # Th17
  "RORC","Th17", "Identity",
  "IL17A","Th17", "Identity",
  "CCR6","Th17", "Identity",
  "IL17F","Th17", "Identity",
  "IL23R","Th17", "Identity",

  # Treg
  "FOXP3","Treg", "Identity",
  "IL2RA","Treg", "Identity",
  "CTLA4","Treg", "Identity",
  "TIGIT","Treg", "Identity",
  "IKZF2","Treg", "Identity",
  "CD27","Treg", "Identity",
  "IL7R","Treg", "Identity",
  
  # CD8 Terminal Effector
  "GZMK","CD8_Eff_Term", "Identity",
  "ZEB2","CD8_Eff_Term", "Identity",
  "EOMES","CD8_Eff_Term", "Identity",
  "TBX21","CD8_Eff_Term", "Identity",
  "PRDM1","CD8_Eff_Term", "Identity",
  "CX3CR1","CD8_Eff_Term", "Identity",
  "FGFBP2","CD8_Eff_Term", "Identity",
  "PRDM1","CD8_Eff_Term", "Identity",
  "KLRC1","CD8_Eff_Term", "Identity",
  "KLRD1","CD8_Eff_Term", "Identity",

  # TPEX (Precursor exhausted)
  "TCF7","TPEX", "Identity",
  "SLAMF6","TPEX", "Identity",
  "CXCR5","TPEX", "Identity",
  "PDCD1","TPEX", "Identity",
  "BCL6","TPEX", "Identity",
  
  # NK
  "NCAM1","NK", "Identity",
  "KLRD1","NK", "Identity",
  "NCR1","NK", "Identity",
  "NCR3","NK", "Identity",
  "KLRC1","NK", "Identity",
  "KLRC2","NK", "Identity",
  "KLRF1","NK", "Identity",
  "TYROBP","NK", "Identity",
  "FCER1G","NK", "Identity"
)

# ADD A NK SIGNATURE !!! (see Bineta) FCGR3A, NCR1, KLRF1, KLRC1, KLRC2, KLRC3, KLRC4, CLEC2B, CLEC4C, NCAM1, CD56, CD3E, CD3D, KLRCE... and more fun !


# ADD Mitochondrial genes to apoptotic signature
```

### Visualization of manual signatures {.tabset}

::: callout-warning
Be careful, a white dot doesn't mean that the gene is not expressed in the cluster, it means that the average expression of the gene in the cluster is equal to the average expression of the gene in all clusters. So if a gene is expressed in all clusters at the same level, it will be white in this plot, even if it is expressed at a high level in all clusters.
:::

#### All {.tabset}

```{r CD8 Viz all signatures together}
#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 10
#| fig-cap: "Average expression of all signature genes across clusters"
#| fig-alt: "Dot plot showing the average expression of signature genes across clusters, with color representing the average expression level and size representing the percentage of cells expressing the gene in each cluster."
#| fig-note: "In this dot plot, each row represents a gene from the signature table, and each column represents a cluster. The color of the dots indicates the average expression level of the gene in that cluster, with red indicating higher expression and blue indicating lower expression. The size of the dots represents the percentage of cells in that cluster that express the gene. This visualization allows us to see which genes are differentially expressed across clusters and to identify potential markers for each cluster based on their expression patterns."

selected_features <- signature_tbl %>%
  pull(feature) %>%
  unique()
  
m2 <- presto_markers %>%
  filter(feature %in% selected_features) %>%
  select(feature, group, avgExpr, pct_in) %>%
  left_join(signature_tbl, by = "feature") %>%
  mutate(signature_class = case_when(
  cell_type %in% c("CD4","CD8","Th1","Th2","Th17","Tfh","Treg") ~ "Identity",
  cell_type %in% c("Cytotoxic","IFN","Inflammatory","HLA") ~ "Function",
  cell_type %in% c("Naif","Memory","Activated","Exhausted",
                    "Resident","Prolif","Senescent","Apoptotic") ~ "State"
)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(cell_type)) %>%
  group_by(feature) %>%
  mutate(
    avgExpr = avgExpr - mean(avgExpr),
    avgExpr = scales::rescale(avgExpr, to = c(-2,2))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(m2, 
       aes(x = feature,
               y = fct_reorder(as.character(group), group),
               color = avgExpr,
               size = pct_in)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient2(low="blue", mid="white", high="red") +
  facet_wrap(~cell_type, scales="free", ncol=10) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, hjust=1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(angle=45, hjust=1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "LT Compartment - All Signatures",
    x = "Genes markers",
    y = "Clusters"
  )

dir.create(
   file.path(
     DIR_RESULT,
     "CD8_Tcells",
     "Annotation",
     "Manual"),
   recursive = TRUE,
   showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "Annotation",
    "Manual",
    "Marker_Signatures_LT_Compartment_All_Signatures.png"),
  width = 20, 
  height = 10)
```

#### Specific {.tabset}

```{r CD8 Viz specific signature}
#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 10
#| fig-cap: "Average expression of specific signature genes across clusters"
#| fig-alt: "Dot plot showing the average expression of signature genes across clusters, with color representing the average expression level and size representing the percentage of cells expressing the gene in each cluster."
#| fig-note: "In this dot plot, each row represents a gene from the signature table, and each column represents a cluster. The color of the dots indicates the average expression level of the gene in that cluster, with red indicating higher expression and blue indicating lower expression. The size of the dots represents the percentage of cells in that cluster that express the gene. This visualization allows us to see which genes are differentially expressed across clusters and to identify potential markers for each cluster based on their expression patterns."

m3 <- presto_markers %>%
  filter(feature %in% selected_features) %>%
  select(feature, group, avgExpr, pct_in) %>%
  left_join(signature_tbl, by = "feature") %>%
  filter(!is.na(cell_type)) %>%
  group_by(feature) %>%
  mutate(
    avgExpr = avgExpr - mean(avgExpr),
    avgExpr = scales::rescale(avgExpr, to = c(-2,2))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

for (i in unique(m3$class)) {
  p <- ggplot(
    m3 %>% filter(class == i),
    aes(x=feature, y=fct_reorder(as.character(group), group), color = avgExpr, size = pct_in)) +
    geom_point()+
    scale_color_gradient2(low = "blue", mid="white",high = "red")+
    # theme_minimal() +
    facet_wrap(~ cell_type, scales = "free", ncol = 4) +
    theme(axis.text.y = element_text(angle=45, hjust=1), 
          axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, hjust=1)) +
    labs(
    title = paste0("LT Compartment - ", i),
    x = "Genes markers",
    y = "Clusters")
  
  print(p)
  
  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(
      DIR_RESULT,
      "CD8_Tcells",
      "Annotation",
      "Manual",
      paste0("Marker_Signatures_LT_Compartment_", i, ".png")),
    plot = p,
    width = 16,
    height = 8)
}
```

### Module scores {.tabset}

We can also help us by calculating module scores for each signature, which will give us a quantitative measure of the expression of each signature in each cluster.

::: callout-tip
This can help us to identify clusters that are enriched for specific signatures and to further characterize their identity and function.
:::

#### Run {.tabset}

```{r CD8 Module score Run}
genes_Activation <- c(
  "CD69", "TNFRSF9", "CD40LG", "NR4A1", "FOS", "JUN"
)

genes_IFN <- c(
  "ISG15", "IFIT2", "IFIT3", "MX1", "STAT1", "IRF7"
)

genes_Inflammatory <- c(
  "NFKBIA", "NFKBIZ", "TNFAIP3", "NFKB1"
)

genes_Prolif <- c(
  "MKI67", "CENPE", "NCAPG", "KIFC1"
)

genes_Th1 <- c(
  "TBX21", "IFNG", "IL12RB2", "STAT4"
)

genes_Th2 <- c(
  "GATA3", "IL4", "IL5", "IL13"
)
  
genes_Th17 <- c(
  "RORC", "CCR6", "IL23R", "IL17A"
)

genes_Treg <- c(
  "FOXP3", "IL2RA", "CTLA4",
  "IKZF2", "TIGIT"
  )

genes_Tfh <- c(
  "BCL6", "CXCR5", "ICOS", "IL21"
)

genes_Cytotoxic <- c(
  "PRF1", "GZMB", "GNLY", "NKG7"
)

genes_Naive <- c(
  "CCR7", "SELL", "TCF7", "LEF1", "IL7R"
)

genes_Resident <- c(
  "CD69", "ITGAE", "CXCR6", "RUNX3"
)

genes_Exhausted <- c(
  "PDCD1", "HAVCR2", "LAG3", "TOX", "ENTPD1", "LAYN"
)

genes_TCM <- c(
  "CCR7", "CD27", "CD28"
)

genes_Senescent <- c(
  "KLRG1", "CDKN1A", "CDKN2A", "TP53"
)

genes_TEM <- c(
  "GZMK", "CXCR3"
  )

genes_TerminalEff <- c(
  "ZEB2", "PRDM1", "EOMES", "CX3CR1", "FGFBP2"
)

genes_TPEX <- c(
  "TCF7", "SLAMF6", "CXCR5", "PDCD1"
)

seu_CD8_QC_Moderate <- AddModuleScore(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate,
  features = list(
    Activation = genes_Activation,
    IFN = genes_IFN,
    Inflammatory = genes_Inflammatory,
    Proliferation = genes_Prolif,
    Naive = genes_Naive,
    TCM = genes_TCM,
    TEM = genes_TEM,
    Cytotoxic = genes_Cytotoxic,
    TerminalEff = genes_TerminalEff,
    Exhausted = genes_Exhausted,
    TPEX = genes_TPEX,
    Treg = genes_Treg,
    Tfh = genes_Tfh,
    Th1 = genes_Th1,
    Th2 = genes_Th2,
    Th17 = genes_Th17,
    Resident = genes_Resident,
    Senescent = genes_Senescent
  ),
  name = "MS_",
  assay = "RNA"
)


# !!! Becareful, respect list order in AddModule
module_names <- names(list(
  Activation = genes_Activation,
  IFN = genes_IFN,
  Inflammatory = genes_Inflammatory,
  Proliferation = genes_Prolif,
  Naive = genes_Naive,
  TCM = genes_TCM,
  TEM = genes_TEM,
  Cytotoxic = genes_Cytotoxic,
  TerminalEff = genes_TerminalEff,
  Exhausted = genes_Exhausted,
  TPEX = genes_TPEX,
  Treg = genes_Treg,
  Tfh = genes_Tfh,
  Th1 = genes_Th1,
  Th2 = genes_Th2,
  Th17 = genes_Th17,
  Resident = genes_Resident,
  Senescent = genes_Senescent
))

old_names <- paste0("MS_", seq_along(module_names))
new_names <- paste0("MS_", module_names)

colnames(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@meta.data)[
  match(old_names, colnames(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@meta.data))
] <- new_names
```

#### Visualize {.tabset}

```{r CD8 Module score Viz 1}
#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 10
#| fig-cap: "Module scores for each signature across clusters - FeaturePlot"

FeaturePlot(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate,
  features = c(
    "MS_Th1",
    "MS_Th2",
    "MS_Th17",
    "MS_Treg",
    "MS_Cytotoxic",
    "MS_Tfh",
    "MS_Naive",
    "MS_Resident",
    "MS_Exhausted",
    "MS_TCM",
    "MS_TEM",
    "MS_TerminalEff",
    "MS_TPEX",
    "MS_Senescent",
    "MS_Proliferation",
    "MS_Inflammatory",
    "MS_IFN",
    "MS_Activation"
  ),
  ncol = 4,
  order = TRUE
)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "Annotation",
    "Manual",
    "Featureplot_Module_score.png"),
  width = 16,
  height = 8)
```

```{r CD8 Module score Viz 2}
#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 10
#| fig-cap: "Module scores for each signature across clusters - Violin plot"

VlnPlot(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate,
  cols = pal,
  features = c(
    "MS_Th1",
    "MS_Th2",
    "MS_Th17",
    "MS_Treg",
    "MS_Cytotoxic",
    "MS_Tfh",
    "MS_Naive",
    "MS_Resident",
    "MS_Exhausted",
    "MS_TCM",
    "MS_TEM",
    "MS_TerminalEff",
    "MS_TPEX",
    "MS_Senescent",
    "MS_Proliferation",
    "MS_Inflammatory",
    "MS_IFN",
    "MS_Activation"
  ),
  group.by = "seurat_clusters",
  pt.size = 0,
  ncol = 4)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "Annotation",
    "Manual",
    "Violinplot_Module_score.png"),
  width = 16,
  height = 8)
```

#### Z-scored {.tabset}

Useful to see which clusters have high scores for which modules, and to help with manual annotation of clusters based on their module score profiles. For example, a cluster with high Th1 module score and low Th17 and Treg module scores might be annotated as a Th1-like cluster, while a cluster with high Treg module score might be annotated as a Treg-like cluster.

```{r CD8 Z-score module Run}
module_means <- seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@meta.data %>%
  group_by(seurat_clusters) %>%
  summarise(
    Activation = mean(MS_Activation),
    IFN = mean(MS_IFN),
    Inflammatory = mean(MS_Inflammatory),
    Proliferation = mean(MS_Proliferation),
    Naive = mean(MS_Naive),
    TCM = mean(MS_TCM),
    TEM = mean(MS_TEM),
    Cytotoxic = mean(MS_Cytotoxic),
    TerminalEff = mean(MS_TerminalEff),
    Exhausted = mean(MS_Exhausted),
    TPEX = mean(MS_TPEX),
    Treg = mean(MS_Treg),
    Tfh = mean(MS_Tfh),
    Th1 = mean(MS_Th1),
    Th2 = mean(MS_Th2),
    Th17 = mean(MS_Th17),
    Resident = mean(MS_Resident),
    Senescent = mean(MS_Senescent)
  )

module_z <- module_means %>%
  mutate(across(
    -seurat_clusters,
    ~ scale(.)[,1]))
```

```{r CD8 Z-score module Viz}
mat <- as.matrix(module_z[, -1])
rownames(mat) <- module_z$seurat_clusters

pheatmap <- pheatmap(
  mat,
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  scale = "none")

ggsave(
  plot = pheatmap,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "Annotation",
    "Manual",
    "Heatmap_Module_Score_Zscore.png"),
  width = 8,
  height = 6)

```

::: callout-warning
Be careful we can compare clusters for a same signature but we can't compare scores between signatures because they are not on the same scale and are not directly comparable. The scores are relative to the expression of the genes in each signature and are not absolute measures of the underlying biology. Therefore, it's important to interpret the module scores in the context of each signature separately and not to compare scores across different signatures directly.
:::

::: callout-tip
Maybe useful too fuse some clusters together based on their module score profiles if they show similar profiles and are not well separated in the UMAP. For example, if two clusters both have high Th1 module scores and low Th17 and Treg module scores, they might represent different subsets of Th1-like cells and could be fused together into a single Th1-like cluster for downstream analyses.
:::

### Choose manual annotations for each cluster {.tabset}

```{r CD8 Choose manual annot}
cluster_annotation_list <- list(
  "0" = "CD8_Exhausted",
  "1" = "CD8_Cytotox",
  "2" = "CD8_Naive",
  "3" = "CD8_Inflammed_Resident_1",
  "4" = "CD8_Activated_Resident",
  "5" = "CD8_Inflammed_Resident_2",
  "6" = "gd_Tcells_1",
  "7" = "gd_Tcells_2"
)

# Add annotations to Seurat object
seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$manual_annotation <- plyr::mapvalues(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$seurat_clusters,
  from = names(cluster_annotation_list),
  to = unlist(cluster_annotation_list))

# output the annotation table
annotation_df <- data.frame(
  cluster = names(cluster_annotation_list),
  annotation = unlist(cluster_annotation_list),
  row.names = NULL
)

knitr::kable(annotation_df, 
             col.names = c("Cluster", "Manual annotation"),
             align = "c")
```
:::::::::::

## Combine automatic and manual annotations {.tabset}

We need to statuate on the annotation of each cluster based on the expression of marker genes, the module scores, and the automatic annotations. We can use all this information to assign a putative identity to each cluster, which will help us to understand the biological functions and relevance of each cluster in the context of our CD8 T cell dataset. The manual annotation can be based on the most enriched markers in each cluster, the module score profiles, and the automatic annotations, but it can also take into account other factors such as the location of clusters in the UMAP space and their relationships with other clusters.

```{r CD8 Combine annot}
seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$consensus_annotation <- case_when(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$seurat_clusters == "0" ~ "CD8_Exhausted",
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$seurat_clusters == "1" ~ "CD8_Cytotox",
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$seurat_clusters == "2" ~ "CD8_Naive",
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$seurat_clusters == "3" ~ "CD8_Inflammed_Resident_1",
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$seurat_clusters == "4" ~ "CD8_Activated_Resident",
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$seurat_clusters == "5" ~ "CD8_Inflammed_Resident_2",
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$seurat_clusters == "6" ~ "gd_Tcells_1",
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$seurat_clusters == "7" ~ "gd_Tcells_2",
  TRUE ~ "Uncertain"
)


annotation_table <- seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@meta.data %>%
  count(seurat_clusters, consensus_annotation, name = "n_cells") %>%
  arrange(as.numeric(as.character(seurat_clusters)))

annotation_table %>%
  kable(col.names = c("Cluster", "Consensus annotation", "Number of cells"),
        align = "c",
        caption = "Final CD8 cluster annotations with cell counts") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE,
                bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"))
```

::: callout-tip
Create a unique col with a consensus annotation between the annotations (automatic mine, automatic PPA, manual mine) to use for downstream analyses and visualization. For example, if the 3 annotations agree on a cell type for a given cluster, we can assign that cell type to the cells in that cluster. If there is disagreement between the annotations, we can assign a more general cell type or keep it as "Uncertain" until we can resolve the discrepancy with further analyses.
:::

<br>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<br>

# Downstream Analysis {.tabset}

## All cells {.tabset}

### Proportion of cells across conditions {.tabset}

```{r CD8 Proportion of cells across conditions}
# See the proportions of cells in each condition
p_prop_celltypes_by_condition <- ggplot(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@meta.data,
  aes(x = condition, fill = manual_annotation)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  labs(
    title = "Proportion of predicted cell types by condition",
    x = "Condition",
    y = "Proportion of cells"
  )
p_prop_celltypes_by_condition

dir.create(
  file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "Downstream_Analysis"),
  recursive = TRUE,
  showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  plot = p_prop_celltypes_by_condition, 
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "Downstream_Analysis",
    "Proportion_celltypes_by_condition.png"),
  width = 12,
  height = 6)

```

## BCL6+ cells

### Define BCL6+ CD8 Tcells

As I dont't have a clear cluster BCL6+, I will create a category of cells that are BCL6+ based on the expression of BCL6 in the CD8 T cells. I will choose a threshold of BCL6 expression to define BCL6+ cells, and then I will visualize the distribution of BCL6 expression to see if there is a clear separation between BCL6+ and BCL6- cells. I will also visualize the distribution of BCL6 expression by condition to see if there are differences in BCL6 expression between conditions. Finally, I will visualize the UMAP colored by BCL6+ vs BCL6- cells to see where the BCL6+ cells are located in the UMAP space.

```{r}
# Visualize via ridgeplot bcl6 EXPRESSION BY CLUSTER
RidgePlot(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, features = "BCL6", group.by = "seurat_clusters") + labs(title = "BCL6 expression by cluster") + theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# See the distribution of BCL6 expression to choose a threshold FeaturePlot( seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, features = "BCL6", reduction = "umap" ) + labs(title = "BCL6 expression in CD8 T cells") + theme_minimal()
df <- data.frame(expr = seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@assays$RNA@data["BCL6", ])
df$condition <- seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$condition

ggplot(df, aes(x = expr, fill = condition)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4) +
  theme_minimal()

VlnPlot(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, features = "BCL6", group.by = "condition")

# Define BCL6+ CD8 T cells based on distribution of BCL6 expression in CD8 T cells threshold_BCL6
treshold_BCL6 <- 0 # This threshold can be adjusted based on the distribution of BCL6 expression
seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$BCL6_status <- ifelse(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate@assays$RNA@data["BCL6", ] > treshold_BCL6, 
  "BCL6_pos", 
  "BCL6_neg")



DimPlot( seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, reduction = "umap", group.by = "BCL6_status", cols = c("grey", "red") ) + labs(title = "BCL6+ CD8 T cells") + theme_minimal()


```

### Frequency of BCL6+ CD8 T cells by condition

```{r BCL6 frequency by condition}
table(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$BCL6_status, seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$condition)

# Visualize the proportion of BCL6+ CD8 T cells by condition
props_BCL6 <-
  ggplot() +
  geom_bar(
    data = as.data.frame(table(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$BCL6_status, seu_CD8_QC_Moderate$condition)),
    aes(x = Var2, y = Freq, fill = Var1),
    stat = "identity",
    position = "fill"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("grey", "red")) +
  labs(x = "Condition", y = "Proportion of BCL6+ CD8 Tcells", fill = "BCL6 status") +
  theme_minimal()
print(props_BCL6)

```

### GSEA pseudo-bulk Analysis

#### Comparison BCL6+ MM vs BCL6+ HD

::: callout-important
This analysis answer the question : In CD8+ BCL6+ cells, are there differences in transcriptional programs between MM and HD ? (Chez les CD8 BCL6+, quels programmes transcriptionnels sont différents entre MM et HD ?)
:::

It's a comparison between conditions (MM vs HD) but only within the BCL6+ compartment. We will create pseudo-bulk samples for each patient and condition, but only for the BCL6+ cells, and then we will run a differential expression analysis between MM and HD in these pseudo-bulk samples. Finally, we will run a GSEA analysis on the results of the differential expression analysis to identify pathways that are differentially regulated between MM and HD in the BCL6+ CD8 T cells. (👉 C’est une comparaison inter-condition, mais à l’intérieur du compartiment BCL6+ uniquement.)

##### DE {.tabset}

Run the differential Expression analysis between MM and HD in the BCL6+ CD8 T cells using edgeR on pseudo-bulk samples. We will create a design matrix for the pseudo-bulk samples, run the DE analysis, and then run GSEA on the results.

###### Design matrix {.tabset}

```{r GSEApseudo-bulk Design matrix}
pseudo_bulk <- AggregateExpression(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate,
  group.by = c("id", "condition", "BCL6_status", "source"),
  assays = "RNA",
  slot = "counts"
)$RNA


# Create metadata for pseudo-bulk sampleseta
colnames_pb <- colnames(pseudo_bulk)
colnames(pseudo_bulk)

parts <- strsplit(colnames_pb, "_")
# parts
meta_pb <- data.frame(
  id = sapply(parts, `[`, 1),
  condition = sapply(parts, `[`, 2),
  BCL6_status = sapply(parts, `[`, 3),
  source = sapply(parts, `[`, 4),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>% 
  mutate(
    id = factor(id),
    source = factor(source),
    condition = factor(condition),
    BCL6_status = factor(BCL6_status)
  )

meta_BCL6pos <- meta_pb[meta_pb$BCL6_status == "BCL6-pos", ]
pseudo_BCL6pos <- pseudo_bulk[, meta_pb$BCL6_status == "BCL6-pos"]

# Vérification patients
table(meta_BCL6pos$id, meta_BCL6pos$condition)
table(meta_BCL6pos$condition)
table(meta_BCL6pos$id)
#### !!!! Chaque colonne du design doit apporter de l’information indépendante. Pas de colinéarité

design <- model.matrix(~ condition, data = meta_BCL6pos)

```

###### Run DE {.tabset}

```{r GSEApseudo-bulk DE}
dge <- DGEList(counts = pseudo_BCL6pos)

# Filtrage
keep <- filterByExpr(dge, design)
dge <- dge[keep, , keep.lib.sizes = FALSE]

# Normalisation
dge <- calcNormFactors(dge)

# Estimation dispersion + fit
dge <- estimateDisp(dge, design)
fit <- glmQLFit(dge, design)

# Test conditionMM : BCL6+ MM vs BCL6+ HD
qlf <- glmQLFTest(fit, coef = "conditionMM")
res <- topTags(qlf, n = Inf)$table
```

##### Ranking {.tabset}

```{r}
ranking <- res$logFC
names(ranking) <- rownames(res)
ranking <- sort(ranking, decreasing = TRUE)
```

##### Gene sets MSigDB {.tabset}

```{r}
msig <- msigdbr(species = "Homo sapiens", category = "H")

pathways <- split(msig$gene_symbol, msig$gs_name)
```

##### GSEA {.tabset}

Let's see if gene are enriched for a specific pathway

###### Run

```{r}
fgseaRes <- fgsea(
  pathways = pathways,
  stats = ranking,
  nperm = 10000
)

fgseaRes %>%
  arrange(padj) %>%
  head()

```

###### Visualize

####### Enrichment Plot - Top pathways

```{r GSEA Enrichment BCL6+ MM vs BCL6+ HD}
dir.create(
   file.path(
     DIR_RESULT,
     "CD8_Tcells",
     "downstream_analysis",
     "gsea",
     "bcl6pos_mm_vs_bcl6pos_hd",
     "enrichment_plot"),
   recursive = TRUE,
   showWarnings = FALSE)

# topN <- fgseaRes$pathway[1:min(5, nrow(fgseaRes))]
fgseaRes_sign <- fgseaRes %>%
  filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(padj)
topN <- fgseaRes_sign$pathway[1:nrow(fgseaRes_sign)]

for (pw in topN) {

  stats_pw <- fgseaRes %>% filter(pathway == pw)

  p <- plotEnrichment(pathways[[pw]], ranking) +
    labs(
      title = pw,
      subtitle = paste0(
        "NES = ", round(stats_pw$NES, 2),
        " | padj = ", signif(stats_pw$padj, 3)
      )
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
  print(p)

  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(
      DIR_RESULT,
      "CD8_Tcells",
      "downstream_analysis",
      "gsea",
      "bcl6pos_mm_vs_bcl6pos_hd",
      "enrichment_plot",
      tolower(paste0("enrichment_plot_", pw, ".pdf"))),
    plot = p,
    width = 8,
    height = 5
  )
}

```

###### Interpretation of the GSEA Enrichment Plot

####### Axes

-   **X-axis (horizontal):**\
    All genes in the ranked list, ordered from the most **upregulated** (left, if sorted in decreasing order) to the most **downregulated** (right).

-   **Y-axis (vertical):**\
    The **cumulative Enrichment Score (ES)**.

The curve represents the running accumulation of enrichment signal as the algorithm walks through the ranked gene list.

::: callout-note
In this case, genes belonging to the pathway are predominantly **downregulated**.
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

####### Enrichment Curve

The running enrichment curve:

-   **Increases** each time a pathway gene is encountered in the ranked list.\
-   **Decreases (or remains flat)** when genes not belonging to the pathway are encountered.

The **maximum (or minimum) deviation from zero** corresponds to the **Enrichment Score (ES)** of the pathway.

::: callout-tip
-   A curve that rises sharply early in the ranking indicates that the pathway is concentrated among the most **upregulated genes**.\
-   Conversely, a curve that drops sharply indicates that the pathway is concentrated among the most **downregulated genes**.
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

####### Tick Marks (Vertical Bars)

Each tick mark along the X-axis represents the position of a pathway gene within the ranked list.

-   If ticks are predominantly on the **left**, the pathway is enriched among **upregulated genes** (here: BCL6⁺ MM).
-   If ticks are predominantly on the **right**, the pathway is enriched among **downregulated genes** (here: HD).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

####### Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)

-   **NES \> 0** → Enrichment at the **top of the ranking** (here: MM).\
-   **NES \< 0** → Enrichment at the **bottom of the ranking** (here: HD).

The magnitude of the NES indicates the strength of the enrichment signal.

####### Multipathways

######## Barplot

```{r GSEA Barplot BCL6+ MM vs BCL6+ HD}
dir.create(
   file.path(
     DIR_RESULT,
     "CD8_Tcells",
     "downstream_analysis",
     "gsea",
     "bcl6pos_mm_vs_bcl6pos_hd",
     "bar_plot"),
   recursive = TRUE,
   showWarnings = FALSE)


topN <- fgseaRes %>%
  filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(padj) %>%
  # slice(1:10) %>%
  mutate(
    log10padj = -log10(padj),
    direction = ifelse(NES > 0, "Up in MM", "Up in HD"),
    pathway_clean = gsub("^HALLMARK_", "", pathway)
  )

p_topN_pathway_barplot <- ggplot(
  topN,
  aes(
    x = reorder(pathway_clean, NES),
    y = NES,
    fill = log10padj
  )
) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "lightgrey",
    high = "red"
  ) +
  labs(
    y = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)",
    x = "",
    fill = "-log10(padj)",
    title = "All significative enriched hallmark pathways",
    subtitle = "BCL6+ MM vs HD"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
p_topN_pathway_barplot

ggsave(
  plot = p_topN_pathway_barplot,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_mm_vs_bcl6pos_hd",
    "bar_plot",
    "bar_plot_all_signif_pathway.png"),
  width = 10, 
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300)

ggsave(
  plot = p_topN_pathway_barplot,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_mm_vs_bcl6pos_hd",
    "bar_plot",
    "bar_plot_all_signif_pathway.pdf"),
  width = 10, 
  height = 6)





topN <- fgseaRes %>%
  # filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(padj) %>%
  # slice(1:10) %>%
  mutate(
    log10padj = -log10(padj),
    direction = ifelse(NES > 0, "Up in MM", "Up in HD"),
    pathway_clean = gsub("^HALLMARK_", "", pathway)
  )

p_all_pathway_barplot <- ggplot(
  topN,
  aes(
    x = reorder(pathway_clean, NES),
    y = NES,
    fill = log10padj
  )
) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "lightgrey",
    high = "red"
  ) +
  labs(
    y = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)",
    x = "",
    fill = "-log10(padj)",
    title = "All enriched Hallmark pathways",
    subtitle = "BCL6+ MM vs HD"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7)
    )
p_all_pathway_barplot

ggsave(
  plot = p_all_pathway_barplot,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_mm_vs_bcl6pos_hd",
    "bar_plot",
    "bar_plot_all_pathway.png"),
  width = 10, 
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300)

ggsave(
  plot = p_all_pathway_barplot,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_mm_vs_bcl6pos_hd",
    "bar_plot",
    "bar_plot_all_pathway.pdf"),
  width = 10,
  height = 6)


```

######## Dotplot

```{r GSEA Dotplot BCL6+ MM vs BCL6+ HD}
dir.create(
   file.path(
     DIR_RESULT,
     "CD8_Tcells",
     "downstream_analysis",
     "gsea",
     "bcl6pos_mm_vs_bcl6pos_hd",
     "dot_plot"),
   recursive = TRUE,
   showWarnings = FALSE)


topN <- fgseaRes %>%
  filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(padj) %>%
  # slice(1:10) %>%
  mutate(
    log10padj = -log10(padj),
    direction = ifelse(NES > 0, "Up in MM", "Up in HD"),
    pathway_clean = gsub("^HALLMARK_", "", pathway)
  )

p_dot_sign_pathway_gsea <- ggplot(
  topN,
  aes(
    x = NES,
    y = reorder(pathway_clean, NES),
    size = log10padj,
    color = NES
  )
) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_color_gradient2(
    low = "blue",
    mid = "white",
    high = "red",
    midpoint = 0
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Normalized Enrichment Score",
    y = "",
    size = "-log10(padj)",
    color = "NES",
    title = "All significative enriched hallmark pathways",
    subtitle = "BCL6+ MM vs HD"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
p_dot_sign_pathway_gsea

ggsave(
  plot = p_dot_sign_pathway_gsea,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_mm_vs_bcl6pos_hd",
    "dot_plot",
    "dot_plot_all_signif_pathway.png"),
  width = 10, 
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300)

ggsave(
  plot = p_dot_sign_pathway_gsea,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_mm_vs_bcl6pos_hd",
    "dot_plot",
    "dot_plot_all_signif_pathway.pdf"),
  width = 10, 
  height = 6)





topN <- fgseaRes %>%
  # filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(padj) %>%
  # slice(1:10) %>%
  mutate(
    log10padj = -log10(padj),
    direction = ifelse(NES > 0, "Up in MM", "Up in HD"),
    pathway_clean = gsub("^HALLMARK_", "", pathway)
  )

p_dot_all_pathway_gsea <- ggplot(
  topN,
  aes(
    x = NES,
    y = reorder(pathway_clean, NES),
    size = log10padj,
    color = NES
  )
) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_color_gradient2(
    low = "blue",
    mid = "white",
    high = "red",
    midpoint = 0
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Normalized Enrichment Score",
    y = "",
    size = "-log10(padj)",
    color = "NES",
    title = "All enriched Hallmark pathways",
    subtitle = "BCL6+ MM vs HD"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7)
    )
p_dot_all_pathway_gsea

ggsave(
  plot = p_dot_all_pathway_gsea,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_mm_vs_bcl6pos_hd",
    "dot_plot",
    "dot_plot_all_pathway.png"),
  width = 10, 
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300)

ggsave(
  plot = p_dot_all_pathway_gsea,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_mm_vs_bcl6pos_hd",
    "dot_plot",
    "dot_plot_all_pathway.pdf"),
  width = 10, 
  height = 6)


```

####### Heatmap of leading edge genes

```{r GSEA Heatmap leading edge genes BCL6+ MM vs BCL6+ HD}
# logCPM une seule fois
logCPM <- cpm(dge, log = TRUE, prior.count = 1)

# annotation colonnes
annotation_col <- data.frame(
  Condition = meta_BCL6pos$condition
)
rownames(annotation_col) <- colnames(logCPM)

# pathways significatifs
fgsea_sig <- fgseaRes %>%
  filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(padj)


gsea_dir <- file.path(
  DIR_RESULT,
  "CD8_Tcells",
  "downstream_analysis",
  "gsea",
  "bcl6pos_mm_vs_bcl6pos_hd",
  "heatmap"
)

dir.create(gsea_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

for (i in seq_len(nrow(fgsea_sig))) {
  
  pw <- fgsea_sig$pathway[i]
  safe_pw <- gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9_]", "_", pw)
  
  leading_genes <- fgsea_sig$leadingEdge[[i]]
  
  # garder seulement gènes présents dans logCPM
  leading_genes <- intersect(leading_genes, rownames(logCPM))
  
  # option : limiter à 40 gènes max
  if (length(leading_genes) > 40) {
    leading_genes <- leading_genes[1:40]
  }
  
  mat <- logCPM[leading_genes, ]
  mat_scaled <- t(scale(t(mat)))
  
  p <- pheatmap(
    mat_scaled,
    annotation_col = annotation_col,
    show_rownames = TRUE,
    cluster_cols = TRUE,
    fontsize_row = 6,
    fontsize_col = 8,
    main = gsub("^HALLMARK_", "", pw),
    silent = TRUE
  )
  p
  
  pdf(file.path(gsea_dir, paste0("heatmap_top40_genes_", safe_pw, ".pdf")),
      width = 8, height = 10)
  grid::grid.newpage()
  grid::grid.draw(p$gtable)
  dev.off()
}

```

######## Enrichment Map

```{r GSEA Enrichment Map BCL6+ MM vs BCL6+ HD}
dir.create(
   file.path(
     DIR_RESULT,
     "CD8_Tcells",
     "downstream_analysis",
     "gsea",
     "bcl6pos_mm_vs_bcl6pos_hd",
     "enrichment_map"),
   recursive = TRUE,
   showWarnings = FALSE)

library(dplyr)

fgsea_sig <- fgseaRes %>%
  filter(padj < 0.05)

# extraire les leading edges
leading_list <- fgsea_sig$leadingEdge
names(leading_list) <- fgsea_sig$pathway


jaccard <- function(a, b) {
  length(intersect(a, b)) / length(unique(c(a, b)))
}

n <- length(leading_list)
sim_matrix <- matrix(0, n, n)
rownames(sim_matrix) <- names(leading_list)
colnames(sim_matrix) <- names(leading_list)

for (i in seq_len(n)) {
  for (j in seq_len(n)) {
    sim_matrix[i, j] <- jaccard(leading_list[[i]], leading_list[[j]])
  }
}

dist_matrix <- as.dist(1 - sim_matrix)
hc <- hclust(dist_matrix, method = "average")



png(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_mm_vs_bcl6pos_hd",
    "enrichment_map",
    "dendrogramme_pathway.png"),
  width = 2000,
  height = 2000,
  res = 300
)

# p_dendro_hallmark <- plot(hc)
# p_dendro_hallmark

dev.off()

p_heatmap_pathways <- pheatmap::pheatmap(sim_matrix,
                   clustering_distance_rows = dist_matrix,
                   clustering_distance_cols = dist_matrix)

ggsave(
  plot = p_heatmap_pathways,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_mm_vs_bcl6pos_hd",
    "enrichment_map",
    "heatmap_pathway.png"),
  width = 8,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300)

ggsave(
  plot = p_heatmap_pathways,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_mm_vs_bcl6pos_hd",
    "enrichment_map",
    "heatmap_pathway.pdf"),
  width = 8,
  height = 6)

```

```{r}
library(igraph)

threshold <- 0

edges <- which(sim_matrix > threshold, arr.ind = TRUE)
edges
edges <- edges[edges[,1] < edges[,2], , drop = FALSE]
edges

if(nrow(edges) > 0) {
  edge_list <- data.frame(
    from = rownames(sim_matrix)[edges[,1]],
    to   = colnames(sim_matrix)[edges[,2]]
  )
  g <- graph_from_data_frame(edge_list, directed = FALSE)
  plot(g)
} else {
  message("Not enough similarity to create edges in the enrichment map.")
}


g <- graph_from_data_frame(edge_list, directed = FALSE)

V(g)$NES <- fgsea_sig$NES[match(V(g)$name, fgsea_sig$pathway)]
V(g)$color <- ifelse(V(g)$NES > 0, "red", "blue")

p_enrichmentmap <- ggraph(g, layout = "fr") +
  geom_edge_link(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_node_point(aes(color = NES), size = 5) +
  geom_node_text(aes(label = name), repel = TRUE, size = 3) +
  scale_color_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red", midpoint = 0) +
  theme_void()
p_enrichmentmap

ggsave(
  plot = p_enrichmentmap,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_mm_vs_bcl6pos_hd",
    "enrichment_map",
    "enrichment_map.png"),
  width = 10,
  height = 10,
  dpi = 300)

ggsave(
  plot = p_enrichmentmap,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_mm_vs_bcl6pos_hd",
    "enrichment_map",
    "enrichment_map.pdf"),
  width = 10,
  height = 10)

```

The enrichment analysis allows us to directly visualize the impact of disease status on transcriptional programs in **CD8 BCL6⁺ T cells**.

We can identify:

-   Pathways associated with the **disease state (MM)** - typically represented in red.
-   Pathways associated with the **healthy condition (HD)** - typically represented in blue.

This addresses the core biological question:

> **In CD8 BCL6⁺ T cells, which transcriptional programs differ between MM and HD?**

Beyond single pathways, we can also observe **clusters of pathways sharing common leading-edge genes**.\
Such clustering suggests coordinated biological processes driven by overlapping gene subsets, potentially reflecting higher-order functional modules rather than isolated pathways.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Comparison: BCL6⁺ vs BCL6⁻

A distinct but complementary analysis consists of comparing **CD8 BCL6⁺** and **CD8 BCL6⁻** T cells *within the same condition* (HD or MM).

This addresses a different question:

> **What is the transcriptional impact of BCL6 expression within a given condition?**

This comparison allows us to:

-   Dissect the intrinsic transcriptional programs associated with BCL6.
-   Determine whether BCL6-dependent programs are conserved across conditions.
-   Identify disease-specific rewiring of BCL6-associated pathways.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Conceptual Distinction

-   **MM vs HD within BCL6⁺** → Effect of disease on a defined cellular subset.\
-   **BCL6⁺ vs BCL6⁻ within HD or MM** → Effect of BCL6 on transcriptional programming in a fixed biological context.

These analyses are methodologically related but biologically distinct. Together, they provide a structured framework to understand the role of BCL6 in CD8 T cells within the disease landscape.

##### In HD

::: callout-important
This analysis answer the question : In Healthy Donors (HD), are there differences in transcriptional programs between BCL6+ cells and BCL6- cells ? (Chez les HD, quels programmes transcriptionnels sont différents entre CD8 BCL6+ et CD8 BCL6- ?)
:::

It's a comparison between BCL6+ and BCL6- cells but only within the Healthy Donors (HD). We will create pseudo-bulk samples for each patient and condition, but only for the HD samples, and then we will run a differential expression analysis between BCL6+ and BCL6- in these pseudo-bulk samples. Finally, we will run a GSEA analysis on the results of the differential expression analysis to identify pathways that are differentially regulated between BCL6+ and BCL6- in the CD8 T cells of Healthy Donors.

###### DE {.tabset}

Run the differential Expression analysis between BCL6+ and BCL6- in the HD condition using edgeR on pseudo-bulk samples. We will create a design matrix for the pseudo-bulk samples, run the DE analysis, and then run GSEA on the results.

####### Design matrix {.tabset}

```{r GSEApseudo-bulk Design matrix BCL6+ vs BCL6- in HD}
pseudo_bulk <- AggregateExpression(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate,
  group.by = c("id", "condition", "BCL6_status", "source"),
  assays = "RNA",
  slot = "counts"
)$RNA


# Create metadata for pseudo-bulk sampleseta
colnames_pb <- colnames(pseudo_bulk)
colnames(pseudo_bulk)

parts <- strsplit(colnames_pb, "_")
# parts
meta_pb <- data.frame(
  id = sapply(parts, `[`, 1),
  condition = sapply(parts, `[`, 2),
  BCL6_status = sapply(parts, `[`, 3),
  source = sapply(parts, `[`, 4),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>% 
  mutate(
    id = factor(id),
    source = factor(source),
    condition = factor(condition),
    BCL6_status = factor(BCL6_status)
  )

meta_HD <- meta_pb[meta_pb$condition == "HD", ]
meta_HD <- droplevels(meta_HD)
levels(meta_HD$id)
pseudo_HD <- pseudo_bulk[, meta_pb$condition == "HD"]

# Vérification patients
table(meta_HD$id, meta_HD$source, meta_HD$BCL6_status)
table(meta_HD$condition)
design <- model.matrix(~ id + source + BCL6_status,
                       data = meta_HD)
qr(design)$rank
ncol(design)
#### !!!! Chaque colonne du design doit apporter de l’information indépendante. Pas de colinéarité


# H22 don't have moelle + blood so we eliminate this patient to have a full design
tab <- table(meta_HD$id, meta_HD$source)
valid_ids <- rownames(tab)[rowSums(tab == 0) == 0]

meta_HD_balanced <- droplevels(meta_HD[meta_HD$id %in% valid_ids, ])
pseudo_HD_balanced <- pseudo_HD[, meta_HD$id %in% valid_ids]

design <- model.matrix(~ id + source + BCL6_status,
                       data = meta_HD_balanced)
qr(design)$rank
ncol(design)

```

###### Run DE {.tabset}

```{r GSEApseudo-bulk DE BCL6+ vs BCL6- in HD}
dge <- DGEList(counts = pseudo_HD_balanced)

# Filtrage
keep <- filterByExpr(dge, design)
dge <- dge[keep, , keep.lib.sizes = FALSE]

# Normalisation
dge <- calcNormFactors(dge)

# Estimation dispersion + fit
dge <- estimateDisp(dge, design)
fit <- glmQLFit(dge, design, robust = TRUE) # We use robust estimation to mitigate the impact of potential outliers, which can be more prevalent in complex designs with multiple covariates and because we have a relatively small number of samples. This approach helps to ensure that our results are not unduly influenced by any single sample or gene, providing a more reliable identification of differentially expressed genes between BCL6+ and BCL6- cells in the HD condition.

# Test  : BCL6+ HD vs BCL6- HD
qlf <- glmQLFTest(fit, coef = "BCL6_statusBCL6-pos")
res <- topTags(qlf, n = Inf)$table
```

###### Ranking {.tabset}

```{r GSEA ranking BCL6+ vs BCL6- in HD}
ranking <- res$logFC
names(ranking) <- rownames(res)
ranking <- sort(ranking, decreasing = TRUE)
```

###### Gene sets MSigDB {.tabset}

```{r GSEA gene sets MSigDB BCL6+ vs BCL6- in HD}
msig <- msigdbr(species = "Homo sapiens", category = "H")

pathways <- split(msig$gene_symbol, msig$gs_name)
```

###### GSEA {.tabset}

Let's see if gene are enriched for a specific pathway

####### Run

```{r GSEA Run BCL6+ vs BCL6- in HD}
fgseaRes <- fgsea(
  pathways = pathways,
  stats = ranking,
  nperm = 10000
)

fgseaRes %>%
  arrange(padj) %>%
  head()

```

####### Visualize

######## Enrichment Plot - Top pathways

Te design is create as BCL6+ vs bcl6- so left side = upregulated in BCL6+

```{r GSEA Enrichment plot BCL6+ vs BCL6- in HD}
dir.create(
   file.path(
     DIR_RESULT,
     "CD8_Tcells",
     "downstream_analysis",
     "gsea",
     "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
     "hd",
     "enrichment_plot"),
   recursive = TRUE,
   showWarnings = FALSE)

# topN <- fgseaRes$pathway[1:min(5, nrow(fgseaRes))]
fgseaRes_sign <- fgseaRes %>%
  filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(padj)
topN <- fgseaRes_sign$pathway[1:nrow(fgseaRes_sign)]

for (pw in topN) {

  stats_pw <- fgseaRes %>% filter(pathway == pw)

  p <- plotEnrichment(pathways[[pw]], ranking) +
    labs(
      title = pw,
      subtitle = paste0(
        "NES = ", round(stats_pw$NES, 2),
        " | padj = ", signif(stats_pw$padj, 3)
      )
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
  print(p)
  
  ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "hd",
    "enrichment_plot",
    tolower(paste0("enrichment_plot_bcl6neg_vs_bcl6pos_gsea_", pw, ".png"))),
  plot = p,
  width = 8,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300)

  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(
      DIR_RESULT,
      "CD8_Tcells",
      "downstream_analysis",
      "gsea",
      "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
      "hd",
      "enrichment_plot",
      tolower(paste0("enrichment_plot_bcl6neg_vs_bcl6pos_gsea_", pw, ".pdf"))),
    plot = p,
    width = 8,
    height = 5)
}

```

####### Interpretation of the GSEA Enrichment Plot

######## Axes

-   **X-axis (horizontal):**\
    All genes in the ranked list, ordered from the most **upregulated** (left, if sorted in decreasing order) to the most **downregulated** (right).

-   **Y-axis (vertical):**\
    The **cumulative Enrichment Score (ES)**.

The curve represents the running accumulation of enrichment signal as the algorithm walks through the ranked gene list.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

######## Enrichment Curve

The running enrichment curve:

-   **Increases** each time a pathway gene is encountered in the ranked list.\
-   **Decreases (or remains flat)** when genes not belonging to the pathway are encountered.

The **maximum (or minimum) deviation from zero** corresponds to the **Enrichment Score (ES)** of the pathway.

::: callout-tip
-   A curve that rises sharply early in the ranking indicates that the pathway is concentrated among the most **upregulated genes**.\
-   Conversely, a curve that drops sharply indicates that the pathway is concentrated among the most **downregulated genes**.
:::

::: callout-note
In this case, genes belonging to the pathway `TNA_A_SIGNALING_VIA_NFKB` & `INTERFERON_GAMMA_RESPONSE` are predominantly **upregulated** while `MYC_TARGETS_V1` & `OXYDATIVE_PHOSPHORYLATION` are predominantly **downregulated**
:::

::: callout-tip
-   A curve that rises (or fall) sharply early in the ranking indicates that the pathway is concentrated among the most upregulated genes and will have a positive NES (or downregulated genes and will havea negative NES). The more the **rise or fall is sharply early**, the more the pathway will be **highly significant** .\
::: 
------------------------------------------------------------------------

######## Tick Marks (Vertical Bars)

Each tick mark along the X-axis represents the position of a pathway gene within the ranked list.

-   If ticks are predominantly on the **left**, the pathway is enriched among **upregulated genes** (here: BCL6+).
-   If ticks are predominantly on the **right**, the pathway is enriched among **downregulated genes** (here: BCL6-).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

######## Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)

-   **NES \> 0** → Pathway enriched among genes upregulated in BCL6+ (vs BCL6−).
-   **NES \< 0** → Pathway enriched among genes upregulated in BCL6− (vs BCL6+).

The magnitude of the NES indicates the strength of the enrichment signal.

######## Multipathways

######### Barplot

```{r GSEA Barplot BCL6+ vs BCL6- in HD}
dir.create(
   file.path(
     DIR_RESULT,
     "CD8_Tcells",
     "downstream_analysis",
     "gsea",
     "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
     "hd",
     "bar_plot"),
   recursive = TRUE,
   showWarnings = FALSE)


topN <- fgseaRes %>%
  filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(padj) %>%
  # slice(1:10) %>%
  mutate(
    log10padj = -log10(padj),
    direction = ifelse(NES > 0, "Up in BCL6+", "Up in BCL6-"),
    pathway_clean = gsub("^HALLMARK_", "", pathway)
  )

p_topN_pathway_barplot <- ggplot(
  topN,
  aes(
    x = reorder(pathway_clean, NES),
    y = NES,
    fill = log10padj
  )
) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "blue3",
    # mid = "lightgrey",
    high = "red3",
    # midpoint = median(topN$log10padj, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) +
  labs(
    y = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)",
    x = "",
    fill = "-log10(padj)",
    title = "All significative enriched hallmark pathways",
    subtitle = "BCL6+ HD vs BCL6- HD"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
p_topN_pathway_barplot

ggsave(
  plot = p_topN_pathway_barplot,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "hd",
    "bar_plot",
    "bar_plot_all_signif_pathway.png"),
  width = 10, 
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300)

ggsave(
  plot = p_topN_pathway_barplot,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "hd",
    "bar_plot",
    "bar_plot_all_signif_pathway.pdf"),
  width = 10, 
  height = 6)




topN <- fgseaRes %>%
  # filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(padj) %>%
  # slice(1:10) %>%
  mutate(
    log10padj = -log10(padj),
    direction = ifelse(NES > 0, "Up in BCL6+", "Up in BCL6--"),
    pathway_clean = gsub("^HALLMARK_", "", pathway)
  )

p_all_pathway_barplot <- ggplot(
  topN,
  aes(
    x = reorder(pathway_clean, NES),
    y = NES,
    fill = log10padj
  )
) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "blue3",
    # mid = "lightgrey",
    high = "red3",
    # midpoint = median(topN$log10padj, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) +
  labs(
    y = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)",
    x = "",
    fill = "-log10(padj)",
    title = "All enriched Hallmark pathways",
    subtitle = "BCL6+ HD vs BCL6- HD"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7)
  )
p_all_pathway_barplot

ggsave(
  plot = p_all_pathway_barplot,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "hd",
    "bar_plot",
    "bar_plot_all_pathway.png"),
  width = 10, 
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300)

ggsave(
  plot = p_all_pathway_barplot,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "hd",
    "bar_plot",
    "bar_plot_all_pathway.pdf"),
  width = 10,
  height = 6)

```

######### Dotplot

```{r GSEA Dotplot BCL6+ vs BCL6- in HD}
dir.create(
   file.path(
     DIR_RESULT,
     "CD8_Tcells",
     "downstream_analysis",
     "gsea",
     "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
     "hd",
     "dot_plot"),
   recursive = TRUE,
   showWarnings = FALSE)


topN <- fgseaRes %>%
  filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(padj) %>%
  # slice(1:10) %>%
  mutate(
    log10padj = -log10(padj),
    direction = ifelse(NES > 0, "Up in BCL6+", "Up in BCL6-"),
    pathway_clean = gsub("^HALLMARK_", "", pathway)
  )

p_dot_sign_pathway_gsea <- ggplot(
  topN,
  aes(
    x = NES,
    y = reorder(pathway_clean, NES),
    size = log10padj,
    color = NES
  )
) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_color_gradient2(
    low = "blue3",
    mid = "lightgrey",
    high = "red3",
    midpoint = 0
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Normalized Enrichment Score",
    y = "",
    size = "-log10(padj)",
    color = "NES",
    title = "All significative enriched hallmark pathways",
    subtitle = "BCL6+ HD vs BCL6- HD"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
p_dot_sign_pathway_gsea

ggsave(
  plot = p_dot_sign_pathway_gsea,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "hd",
    "dot_plot",
    "dot_plot_all_signif_pathway.png"),
  width = 10, 
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300)

ggsave(
  plot = p_dot_sign_pathway_gsea,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "hd",
    "dot_plot",
    "dot_plot_all_signif_pathway.pdf"),
  width = 10, 
  height = 6)





topN <- fgseaRes %>%
  # filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(padj) %>%
  # slice(1:10) %>%
  mutate(
    log10padj = -log10(padj),
    direction = ifelse(NES > 0, "Up in BCL6+", "Up in BCL6-"),
    pathway_clean = gsub("^HALLMARK_", "", pathway)
  )

p_dot_all_pathway_gsea <- ggplot(
  topN,
  aes(
    x = NES,
    y = reorder(pathway_clean, NES),
    size = log10padj,
    color = NES
  )
) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_color_gradient2(
    low = "blue3",
    mid = "lightgrey",
    high = "red3",
    midpoint = 0
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Normalized Enrichment Score",
    y = "",
    size = "-log10(padj)",
    color = "NES",
    title = "All enriched Hallmark pathways",
    subtitle = "BCL6+ HD vs BCL6- HD"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7)
  )
p_dot_all_pathway_gsea

ggsave(
  plot = p_dot_all_pathway_gsea,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "hd",
    "dot_plot",
    "dot_plot_all_pathway.png"),
  width = 10, 
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300)

ggsave(
  plot = p_dot_all_pathway_gsea,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "hd",
    "dot_plot",
    "dot_plot_all_pathway.pdf"),
  width = 10, 
  height = 6)
  
```

######## Heatmap of leading edge genes

```{r GSEA Heatmap leading edge BCL6+ vs BCL6- in HD}
# logCPM une seule fois
logCPM <- cpm(dge, log = TRUE, prior.count = 1)

# annotation colonnes
annotation_col <- data.frame(
  BCL6_status = meta_HD_balanced$BCL6_status
)
rownames(annotation_col) <- colnames(logCPM)

# pathways significatifs
fgsea_sig <- fgseaRes %>%
  filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(padj)


gsea_dir <- file.path(
  DIR_RESULT,
  "CD8_Tcells",
  "downstream_analysis",
  "gsea",
  "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
  "hd",
  "heatmap")

dir.create(gsea_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

for (i in seq_len(nrow(fgsea_sig))) {
  
  pw <- fgsea_sig$pathway[i]
  safe_pw <- gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9_]", "_", pw)
  
  leading_genes <- fgsea_sig$leadingEdge[[i]]
  
  # garder seulement gènes présents dans logCPM
  leading_genes <- intersect(leading_genes, rownames(logCPM))
  
  # option : limiter à 40 gènes max
  if (length(leading_genes) > 40) {
    leading_genes <- leading_genes[1:40]
  }
  
  mat <- logCPM[leading_genes, ]
  mat_scaled <- t(scale(t(mat)))
  
  p <- pheatmap(
    mat_scaled,
    annotation_col = annotation_col,
    show_rownames = TRUE,
    cluster_cols = TRUE,
    fontsize_row = 6,
    fontsize_col = 8,
    main = gsub("^HALLMARK_", "", pw),
    silent = TRUE
  )
  p
  
  pdf(file.path(gsea_dir, tolower(paste0("heatmap_top40_genes_", safe_pw, ".pdf"))),
      width = 8, height = 10)
  grid::grid.newpage()
  grid::grid.draw(p$gtable)
  dev.off()
}

```

######### Enrichment Map

```{r GSEA Enrichment Map BCL6+ vs BCL6- in HD}
dir.create(
   file.path(
     DIR_RESULT,
     "CD8_Tcells",
     "downstream_analysis",
     "gsea",
     "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
     "hd",
     "enrichment_map"),
   recursive = TRUE,
   showWarnings = FALSE)

library(dplyr)

fgsea_sig <- fgseaRes %>%
  filter(padj < 0.05)

# extraire les leading edges
leading_list <- fgsea_sig$leadingEdge
names(leading_list) <- fgsea_sig$pathway


jaccard <- function(a, b) {
  length(intersect(a, b)) / length(unique(c(a, b)))
}

n <- length(leading_list)
sim_matrix <- matrix(0, n, n)
rownames(sim_matrix) <- names(leading_list)
colnames(sim_matrix) <- names(leading_list)

for (i in seq_len(n)) {
  for (j in seq_len(n)) {
    sim_matrix[i, j] <- jaccard(leading_list[[i]], leading_list[[j]])
  }
}

dist_matrix <- as.dist(1 - sim_matrix)
hc <- hclust(dist_matrix, method = "average")



png(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "hd",
    "enrichment_map",
    "dendrogramme_pathway.png"),
  width = 2000,
  height = 2000,
  res = 300
)

# p_dendro_hallmark <- plot(hc)
# p_dendro_hallmark

dev.off()

p_heatmap_pathways <- pheatmap::pheatmap(sim_matrix,
                   clustering_distance_rows = dist_matrix,
                   clustering_distance_cols = dist_matrix)

ggsave(
  plot = p_heatmap_pathways,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "hd",
    "enrichment_map",
    "heatmap_pathway.png"),
  width = 8,
  height = 6)
    

```

```{r GSEA Enrichment Map BCL6+ vs BCL6- in HD - Graph}
library(igraph)

threshold <- 0

edges <- which(sim_matrix > threshold, arr.ind = TRUE)
edges
edges <- edges[edges[,1] < edges[,2], , drop = FALSE]
edges

if(nrow(edges) > 0) {
  edge_list <- data.frame(
    from = rownames(sim_matrix)[edges[,1]],
    to   = colnames(sim_matrix)[edges[,2]]
  )
  g <- graph_from_data_frame(edge_list, directed = FALSE)
  plot(g)
} else {
  message("Not enough similarity to create edges in the enrichment map.")
}


g <- graph_from_data_frame(edge_list, directed = FALSE)

V(g)$NES <- fgsea_sig$NES[match(V(g)$name, fgsea_sig$pathway)]
V(g)$color <- ifelse(V(g)$NES > 0, "red", "blue")

p_enrichmentmap <- ggraph(g, layout = "fr") +
  geom_edge_link(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_node_point(aes(color = NES), size = 5) +
  geom_node_text(aes(label = name), repel = TRUE, size = 3) +
  scale_color_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red", midpoint = 0) +
  theme_void()
p_enrichmentmap

ggsave(
  plot = p_enrichmentmap,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "hd",
    "enrichment_map",
    "enrichment_map.png"),
  width = 10,
  height = 10,
  dpi = 300)

ggsave(
  plot = p_enrichmentmap,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "hd",
    "enrichment_map",
    "enrichment_map.pdf"),
  width = 10,
  height = 10)

```

The enrichment analysis allows us to directly visualize the impact of BCL6 status on transcriptional programs in **CD8 T cells** in the **HD condition**.

We can identify:

-   Pathways associated with the **BCL6+ in HD** - typically represented in red.
-   Pathways associated with the **BCL6- in HD** - typically represented in blue.

This addresses the core biological question:

> **In CD8 T cells of HD, which transcriptional programs differ between BCL6+ and BCL6-?**

##### In MM

::: callout-important
This analysis answer the question : In Multiple Myeloma (MM), are there differences in transcriptional programs between BCL6+ cells and BCL6- cells ? (Chez les MM, quels programmes transcriptionnels sont différents entre CD8 BCL6+ et CD8 BCL6- ?)
:::

It's a comparison between BCL6+ and BCL6- cells but only within the Multiple Myeloma (MM). We will create pseudo-bulk samples for each patient and condition, but only for the HD samples, and then we will run a differential expression analysis between BCL6+ and BCL6- in these pseudo-bulk samples. Finally, we will run a GSEA analysis on the results of the differential expression analysis to identify pathways that are differentially regulated between BCL6+ and BCL6- in the CD8 T cells of Multiple Myeloma.

###### DE {.tabset}

Run the differential Expression analysis between BCL6+ and BCL6- in the MM condition using edgeR on pseudo-bulk samples. We will create a design matrix for the pseudo-bulk samples, run the DE analysis, and then run GSEA on the results.

####### Design matrix {.tabset}

```{r GSEApseudo-bulk Design matrix BCL6+ vs BCL6- in MM}
pseudo_bulk <- AggregateExpression(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate,
  group.by = c("id", "condition", "BCL6_status", "source"),
  assays = "RNA",
  slot = "counts"
)$RNA


# Create metadata for pseudo-bulk sampleseta
colnames_pb <- colnames(pseudo_bulk)
colnames(pseudo_bulk)

parts <- strsplit(colnames_pb, "_")
# parts
meta_pb <- data.frame(
  id = sapply(parts, `[`, 1),
  condition = sapply(parts, `[`, 2),
  BCL6_status = sapply(parts, `[`, 3),
  source = sapply(parts, `[`, 4),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>% 
  mutate(
    id = factor(id),
    source = factor(source),
    condition = factor(condition),
    BCL6_status = factor(BCL6_status)
  )

meta_MM <- meta_pb[meta_pb$condition == "MM", ]
meta_MM <- droplevels(meta_MM)
levels(meta_MM$id)
pseudo_MM <- pseudo_bulk[, meta_pb$condition == "MM"]

# Vérification patients
table(meta_MM$id, meta_MM$source, meta_MM$BCL6_status)
table(meta_MM$condition)
design <- model.matrix(~ id + source + BCL6_status,
                       data = meta_MM)
qr(design)$rank
ncol(design)
#### !!!! Chaque colonne du design doit apporter de l’information indépendante. Pas de colinéarité


# H22 don't have moelle + blood so we eliminate this patient to have a full design
tab <- table(meta_MM$id, meta_MM$source)
valid_ids <- rownames(tab)[rowSums(tab == 0) == 0]

meta_MM_balanced <- droplevels(meta_MM[meta_MM$id %in% valid_ids, ])
pseudo_MM_balanced <- pseudo_MM[, meta_MM$id %in% valid_ids]

design <- model.matrix(~ id + source + BCL6_status,
                       data = meta_MM_balanced)
qr(design)$rank
ncol(design)

```

###### Run DE {.tabset}

```{r GSEApseudo-bulk DE BCL6+ vs BCL6- in MM}
dge <- DGEList(counts = pseudo_MM_balanced)

# Filtrage
keep <- filterByExpr(dge, design)
dge <- dge[keep, , keep.lib.sizes = FALSE]

# Normalisation
dge <- calcNormFactors(dge)

# Estimation dispersion + fit
dge <- estimateDisp(dge, design)
fit <- glmQLFit(dge, design, robust = TRUE) # We use robust estimation to mitigate the impact of potential outliers, which can be more prevalent in complex designs with multiple covariates and because we have a relatively small number of samples. This approach helps to ensure that our results are not unduly influenced by any single sample or gene, providing a more reliable identification of differentially expressed genes between BCL6+ and BCL6- cells in the MM condition.

# Test  : BCL6+ MM vs BCL6- MM
qlf <- glmQLFTest(fit, coef = "BCL6_statusBCL6-pos")
res <- topTags(qlf, n = Inf)$table
```

###### Ranking {.tabset}

```{r GSEA ranking BCL6+ vs BCL6- in MM}
ranking <- res$logFC
names(ranking) <- rownames(res)
ranking <- sort(ranking, decreasing = TRUE)
```

###### Gene sets MSigDB {.tabset}

```{r GSEA gene sets MSigDB BCL6+ vs BCL6- in MM}
msig <- msigdbr(species = "Homo sapiens", category = "H")

pathways <- split(msig$gene_symbol, msig$gs_name)
```

###### GSEA {.tabset}

Let's see if gene are enriched for a specific pathway

####### Run

```{r GSEA Run BCL6+ vs BCL6- in MM}
fgseaRes <- fgsea(
  pathways = pathways,
  stats = ranking,
  nperm = 10000
)

fgseaRes %>%
  arrange(padj) %>%
  head()

```

####### Visualize

######## Enrichment Plot - Top pathways

Te design is create as BCL6+ vs bcl6- so left side = upregulated in BCL6+

```{r GSEA Enrichment plot BCL6+ vs BCL6- in MM}
dir.create(
   file.path(
     DIR_RESULT,
     "CD8_Tcells",
     "downstream_analysis",
     "gsea",
     "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
     "mm",
     "enrichment_plot"),
   recursive = TRUE,
   showWarnings = FALSE)

# topN <- fgseaRes$pathway[1:min(5, nrow(fgseaRes))]
fgseaRes_sign <- fgseaRes %>%
  filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(padj)
topN <- fgseaRes_sign$pathway[1:nrow(fgseaRes_sign)]

for (pw in topN) {

  stats_pw <- fgseaRes %>% filter(pathway == pw)

  p <- plotEnrichment(pathways[[pw]], ranking) +
    labs(
      title = pw,
      subtitle = paste0(
        "NES = ", round(stats_pw$NES, 2),
        " | padj = ", signif(stats_pw$padj, 3)
      )
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
  print(p)
  
  ggsave(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "mm",
    "enrichment_plot",
    tolower(paste0("enrichment_plot_bcl6neg_vs_bcl6pos_gsea_", pw, ".png"))),
  plot = p,
  width = 8,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300)

  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(
      DIR_RESULT,
      "CD8_Tcells",
      "downstream_analysis",
      "gsea",
      "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
      "mm",
      "enrichment_plot",
      tolower(paste0("enrichment_plot_bcl6neg_vs_bcl6pos_gsea_", pw, ".pdf"))),
    plot = p,
    width = 8,
    height = 5)
}

```

####### Interpretation of the GSEA Enrichment Plot

######## Axes

-   **X-axis (horizontal):**\
    All genes in the ranked list, ordered from the most **upregulated** (left, if sorted in decreasing order) to the most **downregulated** (right).

-   **Y-axis (vertical):**\
    The **cumulative Enrichment Score (ES)**.

The curve represents the running accumulation of enrichment signal as the algorithm walks through the ranked gene list.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

######## Enrichment Curve

The running enrichment curve:

-   **Increases** each time a pathway gene is encountered in the ranked list.\
-   **Decreases (or remains flat)** when genes not belonging to the pathway are encountered.

The **maximum (or minimum) deviation from zero** corresponds to the **Enrichment Score (ES)** of the pathway.

::: callout-tip
-   A curve that rises sharply early in the ranking indicates that the pathway is concentrated among the most **upregulated genes**.\
-   Conversely, a curve that drops sharply indicates that the pathway is concentrated among the most **downregulated genes**.
:::

::: callout-note
In this case, genes belonging to the pathway `TNA_A_SIGNALING_VIA_NFKB` & `INTERFERON_GAMMA_RESPONSE` are predominantly **upregulated** while `MYC_TARGETS_V1` & `OXYDATIVE_PHOSPHORYLATION` are predominantly **downregulated**
:::

::: callout-tip
-   A curve that rises (or fall) sharply early in the ranking indicates that the pathway is concentrated among the most upregulated genes and will have a positive NES (or downregulated genes and will havea negative NES). The more the **rise or fall is sharply early**, the more the pathway will be **highly significant** .\
::: 
------------------------------------------------------------------------

######## Tick Marks (Vertical Bars)

Each tick mark along the X-axis represents the position of a pathway gene within the ranked list.

-   If ticks are predominantly on the **left**, the pathway is enriched among **upregulated genes** (here: BCL6+).
-   If ticks are predominantly on the **right**, the pathway is enriched among **downregulated genes** (here: BCL6-).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

######## Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)

-   **NES \> 0** → Pathway enriched among genes upregulated in BCL6+ (vs BCL6−).
-   **NES \< 0** → Pathway enriched among genes upregulated in BCL6− (vs BCL6+).

The magnitude of the NES indicates the strength of the enrichment signal.

######## Multipathways

######### Barplot

```{r GSEA Barplot BCL6+ vs BCL6- in MM}
dir.create(
   file.path(
     DIR_RESULT,
     "CD8_Tcells",
     "downstream_analysis",
     "gsea",
     "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
     "mm",
     "bar_plot"),
   recursive = TRUE,
   showWarnings = FALSE)


topN <- fgseaRes %>%
  filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(padj) %>%
  # slice(1:10) %>%
  mutate(
    log10padj = -log10(padj),
    direction = ifelse(NES > 0, "Up in BCL6+", "Up in BCL6-"),
    pathway_clean = gsub("^HALLMARK_", "", pathway)
  )

p_topN_pathway_barplot <- ggplot(
  topN,
  aes(
    x = reorder(pathway_clean, NES),
    y = NES,
    fill = log10padj
  )
) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "blue3",
    # mid = "lightgrey",
    high = "red3",
    # midpoint = median(topN$log10padj, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) +
  labs(
    y = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)",
    x = "",
    fill = "-log10(padj)",
    title = "All significative enriched hallmark pathways",
    subtitle = "BCL6+ MM vs BCL6- MM"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
p_topN_pathway_barplot

ggsave(
  plot = p_topN_pathway_barplot,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "mm",
    "bar_plot",
    "bar_plot_all_signif_pathway.png"),
  width = 10, 
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300)

ggsave(
  plot = p_topN_pathway_barplot,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "mm",
    "bar_plot",
    "bar_plot_all_signif_pathway.pdf"),
  width = 10, 
  height = 6)




topN <- fgseaRes %>%
  # filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(padj) %>%
  # slice(1:10) %>%
  mutate(
    log10padj = -log10(padj),
    direction = ifelse(NES > 0, "Up in BCL6+", "Up in BCL6--"),
    pathway_clean = gsub("^HALLMARK_", "", pathway)
  )

p_all_pathway_barplot <- ggplot(
  topN,
  aes(
    x = reorder(pathway_clean, NES),
    y = NES,
    fill = log10padj
  )
) +
```
