```{r GSEA Barplot BCL6+ vs BCL6- in MM}
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "blue3",
    # mid = "lightgrey",
    high = "red3",
    # midpoint = median(topN$log10padj, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) +
  labs(
    y = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)",
    x = "",
    fill = "-log10(padj)",
    title = "All enriched Hallmark pathways",
    subtitle = "BCL6+ MM vs BCL6- MM"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7)
  )
p_all_pathway_barplot

ggsave(
  plot = p_all_pathway_barplot,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "mm",
    "bar_plot",
    "bar_plot_all_pathway.png"),
  width = 10, 
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300)

ggsave(
  plot = p_all_pathway_barplot,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "mm",
    "bar_plot",
    "bar_plot_all_pathway.pdf"),
  width = 10,
  height = 6)

```

######### Dotplot

```{r GSEA Dotplot BCL6+ vs BCL6- in MM}
dir.create(
   file.path(
     DIR_RESULT,
     "CD8_Tcells",
     "downstream_analysis",
     "gsea",
     "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
     "mm",
     "dot_plot"),
   recursive = TRUE,
   showWarnings = FALSE)


topN <- fgseaRes %>%
  filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(padj) %>%
  # slice(1:10) %>%
  mutate(
    log10padj = -log10(padj),
    direction = ifelse(NES > 0, "Up in BCL6+", "Up in BCL6-"),
    pathway_clean = gsub("^HALLMARK_", "", pathway)
  )

p_dot_sign_pathway_gsea <- ggplot(
  topN,
  aes(
    x = NES,
    y = reorder(pathway_clean, NES),
    size = log10padj,
    color = NES
  )
) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_color_gradient2(
    low = "blue3",
    mid = "lightgrey",
    high = "red3",
    midpoint = 0
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Normalized Enrichment Score",
    y = "",
    size = "-log10(padj)",
    color = "NES",
    title = "All significative enriched hallmark pathways",
    subtitle = "BCL6+ MM vs BCL6- MM"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
p_dot_sign_pathway_gsea

ggsave(
  plot = p_dot_sign_pathway_gsea,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "mm",
    "dot_plot",
    "dot_plot_all_signif_pathway.png"),
  width = 10, 
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300)

ggsave(
  plot = p_dot_sign_pathway_gsea,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "mm",
    "dot_plot",
    "dot_plot_all_signif_pathway.pdf"),
  width = 10, 
  height = 6)





topN <- fgseaRes %>%
  # filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(padj) %>%
  # slice(1:10) %>%
  mutate(
    log10padj = -log10(padj),
    direction = ifelse(NES > 0, "Up in BCL6+", "Up in BCL6-"),
    pathway_clean = gsub("^HALLMARK_", "", pathway)
  )

p_dot_all_pathway_gsea <- ggplot(
  topN,
  aes(
    x = NES,
    y = reorder(pathway_clean, NES),
    size = log10padj,
    color = NES
  )
) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_color_gradient2(
    low = "blue3",
    mid = "lightgrey",
    high = "red3",
    midpoint = 0
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Normalized Enrichment Score",
    y = "",
    size = "-log10(padj)",
    color = "NES",
    title = "All enriched Hallmark pathways",
    subtitle = "BCL6+ MM vs BCL6- MM"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7)
  )
p_dot_all_pathway_gsea

ggsave(
  plot = p_dot_all_pathway_gsea,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "mm",
    "dot_plot",
    "dot_plot_all_pathway.png"),
  width = 10, 
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300)

ggsave(
  plot = p_dot_all_pathway_gsea,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "mm",
    "dot_plot",
    "dot_plot_all_pathway.pdf"),
  width = 10, 
  height = 6)
  
```

######## Heatmap of leading edge genes

```{r GSEA Heatmap leading edge BCL6+ vs BCL6- in MM}
# logCPM une seule fois
logCPM <- cpm(dge, log = TRUE, prior.count = 1)

# annotation colonnes
annotation_col <- data.frame(
  BCL6_status = meta_MM_balanced$BCL6_status
)
rownames(annotation_col) <- colnames(logCPM)

# pathways significatifs
fgsea_sig <- fgseaRes %>%
  filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(padj)


gsea_dir <- file.path(
  DIR_RESULT,
  "CD8_Tcells",
  "downstream_analysis",
  "gsea",
  "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
  "mm",
  "heatmap"
)

dir.create(gsea_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

for (i in seq_len(nrow(fgsea_sig))) {
  
  pw <- fgsea_sig$pathway[i]
  safe_pw <- gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9_]", "_", pw)
  
  leading_genes <- fgsea_sig$leadingEdge[[i]]
  
  # garder seulement gènes présents dans logCPM
  leading_genes <- intersect(leading_genes, rownames(logCPM))
  
  # option : limiter à 40 gènes max
  if (length(leading_genes) > 40) {
    leading_genes <- leading_genes[1:40]
  }
  
  mat <- logCPM[leading_genes, ]
  mat_scaled <- t(scale(t(mat)))
  
  p <- pheatmap(
    mat_scaled,
    annotation_col = annotation_col,
    show_rownames = TRUE,
    cluster_cols = TRUE,
    fontsize_row = 6,
    fontsize_col = 8,
    main = gsub("^HALLMARK_", "", pw),
    silent = TRUE
  )
  p
  
  pdf(file.path(gsea_dir, tolower(paste0("heatmap_top40_genes_", safe_pw, ".pdf"))),
      width = 8, height = 10)
  grid::grid.newpage()
  grid::grid.draw(p$gtable)
  dev.off()
}

```

######### Enrichment Map

```{r GSEA Enrichment Map BCL6+ vs BCL6- in MM}
dir.create(
   file.path(
     DIR_RESULT,
     "CD8_Tcells",
     "downstream_analysis",
     "gsea",
     "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
     "mm",
     "enrichment_map"),
   recursive = TRUE,
   showWarnings = FALSE)


fgsea_sig <- fgseaRes %>%
  filter(padj < 0.05)

# extraire les leading edges
leading_list <- fgsea_sig$leadingEdge
names(leading_list) <- fgsea_sig$pathway


jaccard <- function(a, b) {
  length(intersect(a, b)) / length(unique(c(a, b)))
}

n <- length(leading_list)
sim_matrix <- matrix(0, n, n)
rownames(sim_matrix) <- names(leading_list)
colnames(sim_matrix) <- names(leading_list)

for (i in seq_len(n)) {
  for (j in seq_len(n)) {
    sim_matrix[i, j] <- jaccard(leading_list[[i]], leading_list[[j]])
  }
}

dist_matrix <- as.dist(1 - sim_matrix)
hc <- hclust(dist_matrix, method = "average")



png(
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "mm",
    "enrichment_map",
    "dendrogramme_pathway.png"),
  width = 2000,
  height = 2000,
  res = 300)

# p_dendro_hallmark <- plot(hc)
# p_dendro_hallmark

dev.off()

p_heatmap_pathways <- pheatmap::pheatmap(sim_matrix,
                   clustering_distance_rows = dist_matrix,
                   clustering_distance_cols = dist_matrix)

ggsave(
  plot = p_heatmap_pathways,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "mm",
    "enrichment_map",
    "heatmap_pathway.png"),
  width = 8,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300)

ggsave(
  plot = p_heatmap_pathways,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "mm",
    "enrichment_map",
    "heatmap_pathway.pdf"),
  width = 8,
  height = 6)

```

```{r}
library(igraph)

threshold <- 0

edges <- which(sim_matrix > threshold, arr.ind = TRUE)
edges
edges <- edges[edges[,1] < edges[,2], , drop = FALSE]
edges

if(nrow(edges) > 0) {
  edge_list <- data.frame(
    from = rownames(sim_matrix)[edges[,1]],
    to   = colnames(sim_matrix)[edges[,2]]
  )
  g <- graph_from_data_frame(edge_list, directed = FALSE)
  plot(g)
} else {
  message("Not enough similarity to create edges in the enrichment map.")
}


g <- graph_from_data_frame(edge_list, directed = FALSE)

V(g)$NES <- fgsea_sig$NES[match(V(g)$name, fgsea_sig$pathway)]
V(g)$color <- ifelse(V(g)$NES > 0, "red", "blue")

p_enrichmentmap <- ggraph(g, layout = "fr") +
  geom_edge_link(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_node_point(aes(color = NES), size = 5) +
  geom_node_text(aes(label = name), repel = TRUE, size = 3) +
  scale_color_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red", midpoint = 0) +
  theme_void()
p_enrichmentmap

ggsave(
  plot = p_enrichmentmap,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "mm",
    "enrichment_map",
    "enrichment_map.png"),
  width = 10,
  height = 10,
  dpi = 300)

ggsave(
  plot = p_enrichmentmap,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "downstream_analysis",
    "gsea",
    "bcl6pos_vs_bcl6neg",
    "mm",
    "enrichment_map",
    "enrichment_map.pdf"),
  width = 10,
  height = 10)

```

The enrichment analysis allows us to directly visualize the impact of BCL6 status on transcriptional programs in **CD8 T cells** in the **MM condition**.

We can identify:

-   Pathways associated with the **BCL6+ in MM** - typically represented in red.
-   Pathways associated with the **BCL6- in MM** - typically represented in blue.

This addresses the core biological question:

> **In CD8 T cells of MM, which transcriptional programs differ between BCL6+ and BCL6-?**

### UCell {.tabset}

To developp and test if interesting signatures are enriched in specific clusters of CD8 T cells. For example, we can test if an IFN-gamma signature is enriched in a specific cluster of CD8 T cells, which could suggest that this cluster is involved in an IFN-gamma response. We can also test if an exhaustion signature is enriched in a specific cluster, which could suggest that this cluster is composed of exhausted CD8 T cells. UCell allows us to calculate module scores for these signatures at the single-cell level and visualize their distribution across clusters and conditions.

::: callout-note
I still need to run it if needed
:::

```{r}
# library(UCell)
# 
# gene_sets <- list(
#   IFN_gamma = c("STAT1","IRF1","CXCL9","CXCL10"),
#   Exhaustion = c("PDCD1","LAG3","TIGIT","HAVCR2")
# )
# 
# seu_CD8 <- AddModuleScore_UCell(
#   seu_CD8,
#   features = gene_sets
# )
# VlnPlot(seu_CD8, features = "IFN_gamma_UCell", group.by = "condition")

```

### SCENIC {.tabset}

To identify transcription factors (TFs) that are active in different clusters of CD8 T cells. GSEA only describe pathways, but not the TFs that regulate these pathways. SCENIC can help us identify which TFs are active in each cluster and how they might be regulating the observed transcriptional programs. SCENIC is more after GSEA (GSEA could also be run in annotation to identify cluster)

::: callout-note
I still need to run it if needed
:::

### Trajectory inference

Maybe not for this project but for future projects, trajectory inference can be used to identify potential differentiation trajectories of CD8 T cells and how they might differ between conditions. For example, we could identify a trajectory of CD8 T cell differentiation from naive to effector to exhausted states, and see if the distribution of cells along this trajectory differs between MM and HD.

::: callout-note
I still need to run it if needed
:::

### InferCNV

Maybe for future projects, inferCNV can be used to identify potential copy number variations (CNVs) in the CD8 T cells, which could be associated with the disease state. For example, we could identify CNVs that are enriched in CD8 T cells from MM patients compared to HD, which could suggest a role for these CNVs in the disease process.

::: callout-note
I still need to run it if needed
:::

## Tfh-like cells {.tabset}

If we had found a real Tfh-like cluster in the annotation step, we could have focused on this cluster to identify specific transcriptional programs associated with Tfh-like cells in MM vs HD, rather than only BCL+ CD8+ T cells. We could have run a GSEA analysis on the Tfh-like cluster to identify pathways that are differentially regulated between MM and HD in these cells. We could also have looked at the expression of specific genes of interest, such as BCL6, to see if they are differentially expressed between conditions in the Tfh-like cluster.

::: callout-note
I still need to run it if needed
:::

### Subset Tfh-like clusters

```{r Tfh subset}
# seu_Tfh <- subset(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, idents = c("2"))
```

### Intra-Cluster gradient

```{r Tfh global FeaturePlot}
# FeaturePlot(seu_Tfh, features = "BCL6", split.by = "condition")
```

```{r Tfh GSEA pseudo-bulk}
#   group.by = c("sample", "condition"),  # patient x condition
#   assays = "RNA",
#   slot = "counts"
# )
# 
# counts <- pb$RNA
```


```{r}
# gene_list <- sign(de$logFC) * -log10(de$PValue)
# names(gene_list) <- rownames(de)
# gene_list <- sort(gene_list, decreasing = TRUE)

```

```{r}
# library(fgsea)
# library(msigdbr)
# library(ggplot2)
# library(dplyr)
# 
# # 1️⃣ Préparer les pathways Hallmark
# msig <- msigdbr(species="Homo sapiens", category="H")
# pathways <- msig %>% split(.$gene_symbol, .$gs_name)
# 
# # 2️⃣ Préparer geneList (named vector, trié décroissant)
# # Exemple : gene_list <- c(GENE1=1.2, GENE2=-0.5, GENE3=2.1)
# gene_list <- sort(gene_list, decreasing = TRUE)
# 
# # 3️⃣ GSEA avec fgsea
# gsea_res <- fgsea(pathways = pathways, stats = gene_list)
# 
# 
# # Filtrer les résultats significatifs
# gsea_sig <- gsea_res %>% filter(padj < 0.05)
# 
# # 4️⃣ Visualisation dotplot simple
# ggplot(gsea_sig %>% top_n(20, wt=NES), 
#        aes(x=NES, y=reorder(pathway, NES), color=padj, size=size)) +
#   geom_point() +
#   scale_color_viridis_c() +
#   theme_minimal() +
#   labs(title="Top Hallmark pathways", x="NES", y=NULL)
# 
# # 5️⃣ Focus BCL6
# bcl6_sets <- msig %>%
#   filter(grepl("BCL6", gs_name, ignore.case = TRUE)) %>%
#   split(.$gene_symbol, .$gs_name)
# 
# gsea_bcl6 <- fgsea(pathways = bcl6_sets,
#                    stats = gene_list)
# 
# # GSEA plot pour un set BCL6
# top_set <- names(bcl6_sets)[1]
# plotEnrichment(bcl6_sets[[top_set]], gene_list) +
#   labs(title = paste("BCL6 activity in Tfh:", top_set))

```

```{r Tfh trajectory analysis}
# library(monocle3)
```

### Save seurat object

```{r Save Seurat object}
qsave(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, file.path(
  DIR_DATA,
  "Processed_data",
  "seu_CD8_QC_Moderate.qs"))
```

### Read Seurat object

```{r Read Seurat object}
seu_CD8_QC_Moderate <- qread(file.path(
  DIR_DATA,
  "Processed_data",
  "seu_CD8_QC_Moderate.qs"))
```

<br>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<br>

# To test

::: callout-note
This represent a list of analyses that I could run if I have more time
:::

## Basic Wilcoxon test {.tabset}

```{r BCL6 expression in Tfh cells}
# Identifier les cellules Tfh
Idents(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate) <- "seurat_clusters"

# Remplace "Tfh" par le nom ou numéro réel de ton cluster Tfh
tfh_cells <- WhichCells(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, idents = "2")

# Extraire l'expression BCL6 pour ces cellules
expr_df <- FetchData(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, vars = c("BCL6", "condition", "id", "source", "emulsion", "sample", "Phase"), cells = tfh_cells)

# Résumé au niveau sample
summary_sample <- expr_df %>%
  group_by(sample, source, condition) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_BCL6 = mean(BCL6),
    median_BCL6 = median(BCL6),
    n_cells = n(),
    pct_BCL6_positive = mean(BCL6 > 0) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(summary_sample)


# Visualisation
for (tissu in unique(summary_sample$source)) {
  df <- summary_sample %>% filter(source == tissu)
  
  stat_test <- df %>%
    rstatix::wilcox_test(pct_BCL6_positive ~ condition) %>%
    rstatix::adjust_pvalue(method = "BH") %>%
    rstatix::add_significance() %>% 
    mutate(y.position = max(df$pct_BCL6_positive) * 1.1)
  
  print(paste("Stat test for", tissu))
  print(stat_test)
  
  p_boxplot_bcl6_pct_source <- ggplot(df, aes(x = condition, y = pct_BCL6_positive)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
    geom_jitter(aes(color = condition), width = 0.15, size = 3) +
    ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(stat_test, label = "{p.adj.signif}\n(p.adj = {p})") +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.2))) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Percentage BCL6+ in Tfh-like cells -", tissu),
      y = "Percentage BCL6+ cells (per sample)",
      x = "Condition"
    ) +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
  print(p_boxplot_bcl6_pct_source)
  
  ggsave(
  plot = p_boxplot_bcl6_pct_source,
  filename = file.path(
    DIR_RESULT,
    "CD8_Tcells",
    "Downstream_Analysis",
    paste0("Percentage_BCL6+_Tfh_cells_by_condition_in_", tissu, ".png")),
  width = 8,
  height = 6)
}
```

Technically, we don't need to compute p.adj because we are only doing one test per tissue, but it's good practice to adjust for multiple testing when we have multiple tissues.

We will see now if MM patients have more BMMC cells thand HD in the Tfh-like cluster, because it could be that the increase in BCL6+ cells in MM is due to an increase in the proportion of Tfh-like cells that are BMMC rather than PBMC, and we want to see if there is an effect of the condition on the percentage of BCL6+ cells within each source (BMMC and PBMC) separately.

```{r}
# See if we have more cells in MM than HD in the Tfh-like cluster
summary_sample %>%
  group_by(sample, source, condition) %>%
  summarise(n_cells = sum(n_cells), .groups = "drop") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = condition, y = n_cells, fill = source)) +
  geom_violin()
```

## Linear model with covariates {.tabset}

```{r BCL6 linear model}
model <- lm(
  pct_BCL6_positive ~ condition * source, 
  data = summary_sample,
  weights = n_cells
)

summary(model)
```

```{r}
anova(model)
```

## BCL6 activity module score {.tabset}

```{r CD8 bcl6 activity module score}
bcl6_targets <- c("CXCR5","PDCD1","ICOS","IL21","SH2D1A")
seu_CD8_QC_Moderate <- AddModuleScore(
  seu_CD8_QC_Moderate,
  features = list(bcl6_targets),
  name = "BCL6_activity"
)

FeaturePlot(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, features = "BCL6_activity1", split.by = "condition")
VlnPlot(seu_CD8_QC_Moderate, features = "BCL6_activity1", group.by = "condition", pt.size = 0)
```

Use `SCTransform` instead of log-normalization for variance stabilization.
